title: Verify SSE survives DigitalOcean's router (App Platform go-live blocker)
description: The whole product is one long-lived text/event-stream per viewer. The Caddy deployment needs 'flush_interval -1' to stop the proxy buffering the stream to death; on App Platform we do not own the edge and cannot set that knob. Unverified: (a) whether DO's router streams text/event-stream unbuffered, (b) whether it reaps an idle-ish connection on a request/idle timeout. If either is wrong, the map silently never updates or drops every N seconds — and no test in this repo can catch it, because it is a property of their infrastructure. VERIFY BY: deploying with ADSB_SOURCE=replay (no feeder needed) and watching /api/stream for >5 minutes for ~1Hz frames. The app already sends Cache-Control: no-cache + X-Accel-Buffering: no (adsb.stream.sse/headers) and a 15s heartbeat, which is everything we can do from our side.
acceptance_criteria: Frames observed arriving at ~1Hz over a deployed App Platform URL, continuously, for at least 5 minutes.
close_reason: VERIFIED against the live deployment 2026-07-12 (live feeder, not replay). 20 s of 'curl -N https://map.bwawan.com/api/stream': one snapshot then an update every ~1.0 s, continuously — no buffering, no batching, no idle-timeout kill. The full chain is browser -> Cloudflare (bwawan.com zone) -> DigitalOcean's edge (itself Cloudflare) -> container, and SSE survives BOTH proxy layers unmodified. Same clean cadence straight at the origin. The feared managed-router buffering does not happen; the Caddy fallback recorded on this bead is not needed.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Knowledge rescued from the Caddyfile before it was deleted (adsb-kh4.7 — the compose+Caddy path is retired; App Platform is the only deployment).

WHAT AN SSE STREAM NEEDS FROM A PROXY, and therefore what to test DO's router for:

1. NO RESPONSE BUFFERING. A proxy that buffers text/event-stream holds frames until its buffer fills, and the map freezes while every health check stays green. Caddy needed an explicit 'flush_interval -1' on the reverse_proxy — it does NOT reliably infer this from Content-Type: text/event-stream. Assume DO's router needs the equivalent and CANNOT be configured, which is exactly why this bead is a go-live blocker rather than a config task.
2. NO COMPRESSION OF THE STREAM. Caddy's 'encode zstd gzip' had to EXCLUDE text/event-stream, or the encoder wraps the stream and frames arrive only when the compressor flushes.
3. NO IDLE TIMEOUT SHORTER THAN THE HEARTBEAT. adsb.stream.broadcast sends a heartbeat; if the platform's idle timeout is shorter, it kills healthy connections and the browser sees a reconnect loop.

HOW TO TEST without a feeder: deploy with ADSB_SOURCE=replay and hold 'curl -N https://<app>/api/stream' open. Frames must arrive ~1/s CONTINUOUSLY. The failure is not an error — it is a silence, or a burst of batched frames, and it looks fine in the logs.

IF DO's ROUTER BUFFERS SSE and cannot be told not to, the fallback is a droplet with a proxy we control. The working Caddy config (flush_interval -1, encode excluding text/event-stream, TLS, XFF) is recoverable at:
    git show 56c85c1:Caddyfile
    git show 56c85c1:compose.yaml

title: Streaming ingest re-stamps every aircraft as heard-now: broken freshness, false spoof flags
description:
On ADSB_SOURCE=sbs|beast deployments, the streaming Sources' fetch path returns accumulator snapshots whose aircraft carry an ABSOLUTE :aircraft/seen-at-ms and NO :aircraft/seen-s (adsb.accumulator/merge-delta stamps seen-at-ms directly). But adsb.aircraft merge-batch/->observation (src/cljc/adsb/aircraft.cljc:87-98) unconditionally overwrites seen-at-ms with captured-at-ms minus (or seen-s 0) — i.e. NOW — on every merge into state (via adsb.state/apply-batch! from main.clj ingest-batch!). REPL-verified: an aircraft truly last heard at t=1s put through merge-batch at t=101s comes out seen-at-ms 101000.

Consequences (streaming deployments only; poll path is correct because aircraft.json provides seen-s):
1. FALSE position-suspect FLAGS: the jump detector judges a resumed track against a state copy wrongly stamped ~1s old — a legitimate 100s reception gap on a moving jet implies ~33,000 kt and flags as spoofed. Any normal gap over ~30s triggers this.
2. Staleness/age-out broken: state shows every aircraft 0s old; connect-time SSE snapshots ship wrong seen-at; client stale-fade never engages from snapshots; server age-out delayed to ~240s (120s accumulator silence + 120s for the frozen-fresh stamp to expire).
3. /api/aircraft/:icao reports a fabricated seen-at-ms.

Fix direction: make merge-batch/->observation respect an already-present :aircraft/seen-at-ms (derive from seen-s/capture only when absent), OR route accumulator snapshots through a merge that preserves absolute stamps. Add a test: an aircraft entering merge-batch with an absolute seen-at-ms and no seen-s keeps its stamp.
acceptance_criteria: On a replayed streaming feed, seen-at-ms in state reflects true last-heard time; a 100s reception gap does not flag position-suspect; age-out fires at the shared threshold; bb test green
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14. Related: the delta path separately skips jump flagging entirely (elaboration pending with owner — no bead yet). Never test against the live feeder; replay a recorded payload.
close_reason: observed-at-ms honours an existing absolute seen-at-ms (7a6de89); streamed snapshots keep their true last-heard stamp. Regression tests: streamed stamp survives merge-batch, streamed silence ages out, and a 100s reception gap across accumulator snapshots no longer flags position-suspect (an impossible hop across the same gap still does). bb test + bb lint green.

title: Investigate: /api/aircraft/:icao leaks receiver-relative rssi
description:
PRIVACY. The aircraft-detail endpoint (src/clj/adsb/http/handlers.clj:30-43) returns the raw domain aircraft, and its response schema (src/cljc/adsb/schema.cljc:78) includes :aircraft/rssi, so reitit coercion keeps it. Verified live: the endpoint returns aircraft/rssi to anonymous clients. The SSE wire deliberately excludes rssi (adsb.wire docstring, adsb-kbm.2) because signal strength is a measurement OF THE RECEIVER: an observer polling this endpoint for every visible aircraft harvests (position, rssi) pairs over time, and rssi-vs-distance regression is a standard receiver-localization technique — the exact invertible class the crop and the r_dst/r_dir strip exist to prevent. broadcast_test.clj:773 asserts no-rssi on the SSE wire; no equivalent test guards this endpoint, which is how it slipped.

INVESTIGATE, then fix: (1) should the endpoint project through wire/aircraft->wire (the audited allowlist) or get its own response schema without rssi/seen-s? (2) sweep every other REST/JSON surface (healthz, stats, any future endpoint) for receiver-relative values that bypass the wire allowlist discipline; (3) quantify exposure: how long has this been live, is the endpoint linked from the UI; (4) add the regex-absence regression test the broadcast suite already has, applied to REST responses.
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14, verified by two independent reads. Privacy memory: receiver/antenna home position is hidden from the public site entirely; rssi is in the same invertible class.

title: Measure real coverage polygon per altitude band; pick crop radius + altitude floor
description:
adsb-au5 shipped the crop MECHANISM; it is configured with REPLACE_ME. Choosing the radius is the part that decides whether it is real or theatre.

The crop must sit strictly inside true coverage in EVERY bearing, or the shortfall in the weakest bearing leaks the geometry back. So: record a few hours off the feeder, bucket detections by (bearing, altitude band), find the real coverage polygon, inscribe the disc.

Radius and altitude floor trade off through the radio horizon (~1.23*sqrt(ft) nm). A wide crop needs a high floor before coverage is uniform across it; a tight crop keeps the low traffic. Pick both together.

This also DEFANGS adsb cadence leak (see the quantizer bead): uniform coverage inside the crop is what makes update-rate a weak gradient rather than a sharp edge. Terrain notches inside the crop are the sharp, high-SNR residual.

title: Privacy crop gate: publish only aircraft inside a declared disc
description:
The wire allowlist hides receiver-relative FIELDS, but the observation set itself is a portrait of the antenna: the union of published positions is a disc centred on the roof, and low-altitude coverage plus terrain notches sharpen it.

Fix: crop to a fixed, DECLARED disc (ADSB_CROP_LAT/LON/RADIUS_KM) that sits strictly inside true coverage in every bearing. The published boundary then reveals the crop, which we announce, instead of the horizon.

Distinct from plausibility/gate-range, which stays: that one is receiver-centred and anti-spoofing (reject tracks that cannot have come from this antenna's sky). The crop is privacy and is centred on a decoy point, never on the receiver.

Applied at ingest, before state/apply-batch!, so nothing outside the crop ever enters the picture -> never reaches wire, stats, snapshot, or any future endpoint.

title: Real-time stream: push each aircraft delta to SSE clients the instant the server applies it
description:
Owner requirement (2026-07-14, supersedes any coalescing proposal): every aircraft update reaches the browser AS SOON AS the server has it — no 1 Hz sampling, no coalesce window; one aircraft per SSE event is acceptable and expected. Today the pipeline is two stacked unsynchronized 1 Hz samplers (poll loop fetch! + broadcast tick), so a track waits 0-2 s; and every tick resends the full picture regardless of change.

Shape of the fix:
- EVENT UNIT: the aircraft's full merged post-accumulate state (an upsert keyed by :aircraft/icao), NOT a field diff. Upserts are idempotent, so a lost frame heals on that aircraft's next message (~0.5 s) — no sequencing or replay protocol.
- SOURCE HOOK: streaming Sources (sbs, beast-source) gain an optional on-delta hook (opts fn or an optional protocol alongside Metadata) fired after each accumulate with the merged aircraft. INGEST MUST NEVER BLOCK ON FAN-OUT: hand off through a bounded queue/executor to the broadcaster; if the queue is full, drop deltas (the periodic reconcile heals) rather than stall the reader thread.
- RECONCILE + REMOVALS: keep a low-rate full-picture update (the existing tick, relaxed to ~10 s) so age-out removals and any missed upserts converge; age-out sweep keeps running on the scheduler regardless of audience. Snapshot-on-connect unchanged.
- POLL SOURCE: unchanged semantics (its cadence is inherent to polling); the wire protocol must let a client handle full-picture updates and single-aircraft upserts uniformly.
- STATS/FEEDER health: computed on the periodic frame only, never per-delta.
- SLOW-CONSUMER policy: re-argue the ns docstring honestly (the 'every frame is the full picture' invariant is gone); drop-and-close on failed send! stays; note the bounded per-delta frame size and reconcile as the new bounds.
- FRONTEND: stream.cljs handles the upsert event (merge into the client picture by icao); the map layer already pushes the full cached picture per real frame — an upsert updates the cache then pushes. NO smoothing/interpolation — owner decision no-predicted-motion-owner-decision-2026-07-14 stands; rendering each event immediately is the point.
- LATENCY PROOF: an end-to-end test asserting a delta applied server-side is on a client channel well under 100 ms (no tick boundary in the path).
acceptance_criteria: A message arriving at a streaming Source reaches every ready SSE client without waiting on any fixed tick (test proves sub-100ms, no 1 Hz boundary); events carry one full merged aircraft upsert; reconcile frame handles removals/convergence and carries stats; poll source behavior unchanged; ingest reader threads never block on fan-out (bounded handoff, drop-oldest under pressure, tested); browser applies upserts immediately and cljs suite covers it; slow-consumer docstring re-argued; full bb test and scoped lint pass.
notes: 2026-07-14 owner design change (supersedes the reconcile portion of the description): NO periodic full-picture reconcile frame. Aircraft data flows ONLY as snapshot-on-connect + per-delta upserts (streaming) or the 1 Hz update tick (poll). Stats + feeder health move to a DEDICATED 'stats' SSE event (~10s, configurable; one sent immediately after the snapshot on connect), never mixed into aircraft frames — rationale: mixing real data and stats in one frame couples them; if one changes the other must be affected; separated, changing one is unlikely to break the other. Applies to ALL frames: snapshot/update carry aircraft only; wire/picture->wire loses stats/feeder; the stats event gets its own wire shape. Removals converge via client-side age-out (layer tick, shared threshold); server age-out sweep keeps running on the scheduler for its own picture.

title: Garden watch is wedged from its first recompile: :reload-all regenerates garden's record classes
description: adsb.css.build/watch! requires adsb.css.app with :reload-all, which reloads garden.types too: defrecord CSSAtRule compiles a NEW class each pass, while garden.compiler (loaded once via adsb.css.build, never itself reloaded) keeps its protocols extended on the ORIGINAL class. Every at-media record created after the first reload falls through IExpandable dispatch into the declaration branch, where (empty record) throws 'Can't create empty: garden.types.CSSAtRule'. Every recompile in a watch session fails; manual bb css runs masked it. Fix: reload only the adsb.css.* tree — load-file the sources in dependency order read from their own ns forms; garden classes stay one generation forever.
close_reason: watch! now load-files the adsb.css.* sources in dependency order (topo-sorted from each file's own ns form; build.clj excluded) instead of require :reload-all — garden's record classes stay one generation for the life of the JVM. Verified: standalone watch survived three consecutive recompiles where the old loop died on its first change. css_test pins the load order. NOTE: the watch inside the currently-running bb dev still runs the old code and stays wedged until bb dev is restarted. Commit 890d7f3.

title: Expose SBS/Beast over the tunnel: Access-gate the TCP hostnames, cloudflared sidecar, endpoint config
description:
sbs.bwawan.com and beast.bwawan.com ingress rules were created 2026-07-13 (tcp:// to ultrafeeder 30003/30005) — but WITHOUT an Access policy yet. GATE FIRST, before anything else in this bead: a TCP hostname with no Access policy is reachable by anyone via 'cloudflared access tcp' with no credentials. Same model as adsb-sil: Access self-hosted apps for both hostnames, action Service Auth (not Allow), the service token as the ONLY Include rule, no identity principal. Verify the ingress catch-all is still http_status:404 and one-service-wide.

Then the cloud side: raw TCP through the tunnel requires a client-side cloudflared ('cloudflared access tcp --hostname sbs.bwawan.com --url 127.0.0.1:30003', service token via TUNNEL_SERVICE_TOKEN_ID/SECRET) — the DO App Platform container must run cloudflared as a sidecar process beside the JVM (entrypoint starts both; see .do/app.yaml and the image build). The SBS/Beast Sources just dial host:port; add config so prod points at the local sidecar listeners and dev points at the LAN feeder directly — same both-or-neither spirit as ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_ID/SECRET. The CF-Access header code in adsb.ingest.config is HTTP-only and does not apply here.

Note for the Source: through the sidecar, a dead tunnel can look like a healthy-but-silent local socket — the Source's staleness handling (no bytes for N seconds = dead) is what surfaces it; verify that behavior end-to-end when wiring this up.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated 'cloudflared access tcp' to both hostnames is refused; with the service token it connects; ingress catch-all still 404; cloudflared sidecar runs in the app container and survives restart; prod config dials sidecar listeners, dev dials LAN; a stalled tunnel is detected by the Source as stream death; no receiver-position data exposed.
notes:
adsb-0in landed (commit 6f72494) with (sbs/->source host port) and opts {:connect-timeout-ms :reconnect-ms :clock} but deliberately did NOT wire config: ADSB_SOURCE-style selection (poll vs sbs vs beast) and host/port env (e.g. ADSB_SBS_HOST/ADSB_SBS_PORT) are still unwired. That wiring belongs here, alongside the prod-dials-sidecar vs dev-dials-LAN split this bead already owns. The Source's unreachable semantics: fetch! throws ::unreachable while disconnected; a connected-but-silent socket returns an aging snapshot — verify the stalled-tunnel case maps to one of those two, not a third.
Tunnel TCP verified WORKING end-to-end 2026-07-14: Access apps live on sbs/beast.bwawan.com (anonymous 403, service token accepted), and a raw websocket upgrade (HTTP/1.1, CF-Access-Client-Id/Secret headers) streams live data — 101 Switching Protocols, SBS CSV and Beast binary both flowing. CRITICAL FINDING for the sidecar plan: 'cloudflared access tcp' (client 2026.7.1) consistently fails against these hostnames — connection closed mid-upgrade, zero bytes — while the raw websocket path works perfectly. So the planned cloudflared sidecar may be unnecessary AND unreliable: the JVM can dial wss://sbs.bwawan.com directly with the two Access headers (java.net.http WebSocket or hato), unwrap websocket frames, and feed the existing Sources — no second process, no sidecar supervision, reusing the same ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_ID/SECRET env pair the HTTP poller already holds. Weigh 'websocket transport wrapper around the TCP Sources' vs debugging the cloudflared client. Real 30s captures of both feeds were taken via this path for adsb-c75.
adsb-elf IMPLEMENTED (commit 0ff131f) — wss transport, NOT the cloudflared sidecar. Left OPEN for the host's live smoke check.

Shape built:
- adsb.ingest.tcp: transport seam. reader-state opts now take :transport (host port {:connect-timeout-ms :idle-timeout-ms}) -> {:in InputStream :close!}, default tcp/socket-transport (plain socket, unchanged). Pumps read a plain InputStream; serve-connection! owns dial/close. Renamed the socket atom to :connection. Added :idle-timeout-ms (default 60000).
- adsb.ingest.wss (NEW): java.net.http websocket transport. build-websocket! adds CF-Access headers, blocks for the upgrade. Listener copies binary/text messages to a queue; consumer-driven demand (request(1) as each message is drained) bounds buffering to ~1 message with no callback blocking; onPing/onPong re-request + auto-Pong. queue-input-stream drains with an idle-timeout poll that throws SocketTimeoutException on silence.
- Stall detection uniform across transports: socket via SO_TIMEOUT, wss via queue poll; both throw out of read -> serve-connection! reconnect -> persistent silence => connected?=false, fetch! throws ::unreachable.
- config: source-kind classifies ADSB_SOURCE poll(default)|sbs|beast|replay (unknown fails fast). parse-feed-url parses ADSB_FEED_URL: tcp://host:port -> {:scheme :tcp}, wss://host[:port] -> {:scheme :wss :uri} (default 443); blank/malformed/wrong-scheme/portless-tcp fail fast naming the env var.
- main/build-source! wires :poll/:replay/:sbs/:beast; wss feeds get {:transport (wss/transport uri (feeder-auth-headers env))} (both-or-neither auth reused).
- .do/app.yaml documents ADSB_SOURCE + ADSB_FEED_URL (placeholders; wss://sbs.bwawan.com + existing auth pair = prod shape).

Tests: bb test:clj green (211 tests / 1284 assertions), bb lint clean. New: wss_test (captures over wss == over socket byte-for-byte; wss stall reconnect; unreachable throws), sbs_test socket-stall reconnect, config_test source-kind + parse-feed-url, main_test feed-url capture + fail-fast. No live feeder touched; in-process http-kit ws server + committed captures only.

HOST LIVE SMOKE (only the host may hit the real tunnel), at the JVM REPL:
(require '[adsb.env :as env] '[adsb.ingest.config :as config] '[adsb.ingest.wss :as wss] '[adsb.ingest.sbs :as sbs] '[adsb.ingest.source :as source])
(import '(java.net URI))
(let [headers (config/feeder-auth-headers (env/read!))
      src (source/open! (sbs/->source \"sbs.bwawan.com\" 443
                          {:transport (wss/transport (URI. \"wss://sbs.bwawan.com\") headers)}))]
  (Thread/sleep 8000)
  (let [n (count (source/fetch! src))] (source/close! src) n))
;; expect a positive aircraft count. Beast: beast-source/->source + wss://beast.bwawan.com. A 403/ClassOfError from build-websocket! means the token/headers did not satisfy Access.
close_reason: wss transport landed (0ff131f) and live-verified by host 2026-07-14: SBS over wss://sbs.bwawan.com → 21 aircraft/16 positions in 8s; Beast over wss://beast.bwawan.com → 18 aircraft/14 CPR-decoded positions in 10s, cross-consistent with the SBS run (same flights, physically continuous tracks). Access gating verified (anonymous 403, token 101). No sidecar. Residual: ingress catch-all 404 verification remains a dashboard item under adsb-sil.
