title: Sticky on-ground? on the SBS path: airborne aircraft display GND
description:
:aircraft/on-ground? is a true-or-absent marker, and the accumulator's contract (absent fields persist) is silently wrong for it. SBS ->on-ground? (src/clj/adsb/ingest/sbs.clj:159-164) returns nil for airborne (IsOnGround 0 or empty), so the airborne delta carries NO on-ground? key — and accumulator/merge-delta's plain merge (src/cljc/adsb/accumulator.cljc:32) retains a stale true forever. No delta can ever clear it because the domain marker cannot express airborne.

REPL-reproduced: accumulate on-ground true, then accumulate altitude 38000 + position -> state is on-ground true at FL380. The contradiction rides the wire (aircraft->wire emits :on-ground true), and the UI PRIORITIZES it over altitude: aircraft_panel.cljs:96-99, roster.cljs:68-70, map/emergency.cljs:116 all show GND/ground for a departed aircraft climbing out.

Scope: only the SBS streaming path. Beast never emits on-ground?; the poll path recomputes per snapshot.

Fix direction (pick one): have SBS ->on-ground? emit an EXPLICIT false for airborne so the merge overwrites (schema already permits boolean false; keeps the accumulator a pure merge — preferred), or special-case on-ground? in merge-delta (drop when the incoming delta reasserts airborne altitude/position).
acceptance_criteria: An SBS aircraft that reports on-ground then sends airborne messages shows its altitude, not GND, in panel/roster/emergency; regression test at the accumulator or coercion level; bb test green

title: Streaming reader state never evicts: picture atom and cpr-state grow unbounded
description:
Two unbounded growths in the streaming reader, plus one correctness side effect.

(a) The TCP reader's picture atom (src/clj/adsb/ingest/tcp.clj:254, accumulate! at 122-136) never removes entries: accumulator/snapshot FILTERS aged-out aircraft per fetch but the atom keeps every ICAO ever heard for process lifetime. A busy site hears thousands of unique airframes per day — unbounded heap growth over weeks of uptime.

(b) cpr-state (src/cljc/adsb/ingest/mode_s.cljc:354-409) prunes stale halves/references only when the SAME icao is heard again; there is no global sweep. REPL-verified: an entry stamped t=0 survives untouched at t of ~11.5 days because its aircraft was never re-processed. Resets on reconnect, so bounded only by distinct-ICAO count per connection — tens of MB over a week on a healthy connection.

(c) Correctness side effect of (a): an aircraft returning after hours whose first messages are position-less (callsign/altitude before CPR pairs) merges into its retained ancient entry — its HOURS-OLD position is broadcast via the delta path stamped heard-now, and outside-crop? treats the merged aircraft as positioned so it sails through admit.

Fix direction: periodic sweep at the state owners — drop aged-out? entries from the picture atom (every N folds or on a timer), sweep cpr-state entries whose newest timestamp is past the age-out threshold (reuse fresh-at?/prune-cpr-entry, dissoc entries that prune empty), and/or have merge-delta discard inherited fields older than the age-out threshold. Keep the pure domain pure — sweeps live in the impure owners (tcp.clj / beast_source.clj).
acceptance_criteria: Reader picture atom and cpr-state hold no entries past the age-out threshold after a sweep; a returning aircraft does not inherit an hours-old position; regression tests with literal clocks; bb test green
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14, (b) and (c) REPL-verified.
close_reason: Fixed in da2db46. Picture atom and cpr-state both sweep on a 60s interval (accumulator/sweep + tcp/sweep-picture!; mode-s/sweep-cpr-state + the Beast loop's sweep-cpr-state-if-due), and merge-delta treats an aged-out previous as nil so a returning aircraft inherits no hours-old position. Regression tests with literal clocks in accumulator_test, mode_s_test, the new tcp_test, and beast_source_test. bb test green (236 clj + 219 cljs), bb lint clean.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Fix implemented and REPL-verified; bb test:clj + bb lint green. Three parts: (1) accumulator/sweep + tcp/sweep-picture! evicts aged-out entries from the reader's picture atom on a 60s interval, swept before the fold; (2) mode-s/sweep-cpr-state + the Beast read loop's sweep-cpr-state-if-due drops CPR entries whose halves and reference have all aged out; (3) accumulator merge-delta treats an AGED-OUT previous as nil, so a returning aircraft cannot inherit an hours-old position — this makes (c) deterministic rather than dependent on whether a sweep happened to run.

NOTE: the existing accumulator_test 'a delta revives an aircraft that had aged out' asserted the pre-silence callsign carries through revival. That contract IS bug (c); rewritten to the new one.

COMMIT BLOCKED: src/clj/adsb/ingest/tcp.clj also holds adsb-b36's uncommitted in-flight work (plausibility jump flagging), so this cannot be committed scoped to its own files without dragging that in. Awaiting the host.

title: Investigate: streaming upsert render path — optimization options that keep per-message cadence
description:
EXPLORATION / collaboration bead, not a prescribed fix. On the streaming deployment every per-aircraft SSE upsert replaces :aircraft/picture, and the aircraft-layer track then rebuilds the ENTIRE FeatureCollection (geo conversion over all aircraft), re-folds trail history, runs two full clj->js conversions, and issues two full setData calls (each a geojson-vt reindex of the whole source) — at up to one flush per animation frame under a busy feed (~60 msgs/s), for a sky where each aircraft only changes ~2x/s.

CONSTRAINT (owner, 2026-07-14): do NOT solve this by reducing the message/push rate — no throttle, no coalesce window, no smoothing (see also adsb-a4g: no predicted motion). The per-upsert cadence is the product. The question is how to make each push cheaper, not rarer.

Option space to evaluate (agent should benchmark/prototype against a replayed payload, never the live feeder):
1. Incremental source updates: MapLibre GeoJSON sources support updateData (partial add/update/remove diffs) since v3 — push only the one changed aircraft feature + its trail instead of wholesale setData. Likely the biggest win; check interaction with lineMetrics on the trail source.
2. Cache per-aircraft Feature objects keyed by aircraft map identity (persistent-data equality makes unchanged aircraft hit the cache), so the per-push conversion is O(changed) not O(sky) even if setData stays wholesale.
3. Skip the clj->js wall: build/maintain the FeatureCollection as a mutable JS structure at the seam (mutate one feature per upsert), so no full-tree conversion per push.
4. Trails: accumulate is already O(sky) per push and rebuilds the history map — make it incremental on the single upserted icao (the full-picture snapshot path keeps the fold).
5. Source tuning: buffer/tolerance/cluster options on the geojson sources to cut reindex cost; measure whether geojson-vt reindex is actually the dominant term before micro-tuning.
6. Escalation path if 1-5 underdeliver: custom WebGL layer or symbol layout via feature-state to decouple geometry updates from style re-evaluation.

Deliverable: measurements (frame time / CPU per push at realistic message rates and sky sizes, replayed data), a recommendation with tradeoffs, and a follow-up implementation bead. Files: src/cljs/adsb/map/aircraft_layer.cljs (push!), src/cljs/adsb/map/maplibre.cljs (set-source-data! seam), src/cljc/adsb/trails.cljc, src/cljc/adsb/geo.cljc, src/cljs/adsb/stream.cljs (:stream/upsert).
notes: Raised by PENNY perf audit 2026-07-14. Related: adsb-3kf (trails backing-vector leak — fix lands first; option 4 touches the same fold). Owner explicitly wants options presented, worked as a collaboration with an agent, before any code change.

title: Trail rings leak: subvec trim retains the whole backing vector
description:
adsb.trails/append-position caps a ring with (subvec grown (- n max-positions)), but conj on a Subvec appends to the BACKING vector and re-wraps — the backing grows by one entry per append and is never reclaimed while the aircraft stays in the picture. The documented memory bound ('max-positions x live aircraft, does not grow with time') is false for any long-dwelling moving aircraft (loitering helicopters, pattern traffic): memory grows linearly with dwell time, in every connected browser, and on the JVM wherever the same fold runs.

VERIFIED empirically in both runtimes (PENNY audit, 2026-07-14): 10,000 distinct positions through append-position -> ring counts 60, backing vector holds all 10,000 position maps. JVM REPL via reflection on APersistentVector$SubVector.v; browser via shadow REPL (.-v ring) on cljs.core/Subvec. Same structure in both: Subvec -conj does (-assoc-n v end o) on the backing.

Fix: materialize the trim — e.g. (into [] (subvec grown (- n max-positions))) or (vec (rest grown)) — so the dropped head is actually released. O(60) copy per append on a changed position, negligible at feed cadence. Add a test asserting the ring is NOT a subvec (or that its backing does not grow) after > max-positions appends.
acceptance_criteria: append-position never retains more than max-positions positions reachable from the returned ring; regression test proves the backing does not grow past the cap; bb test green
close_reason: Materialized the subvec trim in append-position (commit 455cd33). Verified in the JVM REPL by reflecting on APersistentVector$SubVector.v: 10,000 appends retained 10,000 positions before, 60 after, with the visible ring identical. Regression test append-actually-releases-the-positions-it-drops asserts the trimmed ring is not a subvec view in both runtimes — a count assertion is what the original bug walked past. bb test green (213 tests / 1571 assertions), bb lint clean.

title: Roster dock opens CLOSED; edge-arrow inset measures the rail instead of assuming it
description:
Default the roster drawer to :closed — the map is the product and gets the viewport until the reader asks for the roster.

Named the default once (adsb.ui.roster/default-sheet) instead of repeating :half as a get-fallback in three places. Kept a separate default-open-sheet (:half) for where the sheet lands when OPENED without a height named — conflating 'where it starts' with 'where it opens to' is what makes a drawer that cannot be shut.

Shook out a latent bug: adsb.map.emergency hardcoded roster-px 300 as the desktop edge-arrow inset, but .adsb-roster is 300px open and 40px shut (adsb.css.roster). Closing the drawer already mis-inset the arrow by 260px into empty map; defaulting to closed made it wrong on EVERY load. Now measured from the rendered rail (roster-width-px), so the arrow follows the dock open and shut on the next sync.
close_reason: Closed

title: Draw the declared crop boundary on the map (config event + line layer)
description:
Deliver the crop centre+radius to the frontend and stroke the published-coverage boundary on the chart.

Wire: new connect-time 'config' SSE event, sent ONCE ahead of the snapshot (static — nothing on it can change while the process lives). adsb.wire/crop->wire + wire->crop.

Safe on the wire precisely BECAUSE the centre is the declared decoy, not the antenna. Guard: a disabled crop emits NO coordinate and must never fall back to the receiver position — asserted in broadcast_test wire-privacy.

Map: adsb.map.crop draws a GeoJSON Polygon ring (adsb.geo/circle, 128 vertices via destination on the true great-circle rim) stroked by a 2px dashed line layer. NOT MapLibre's native circle layer — its radius is in screen pixels and would swell/shrink against the ground under zoom.
close_reason: Closed

title: Quantize per-aircraft emission cadence (privacy: update rate is a proxy for RSSI)
description:
Message rate per aircraft falls off with distance from the antenna, and distant aircraft drop out and reappear while near ones never do. Since adsb-jpf we push per-delta in real time, so that gap structure passes straight through to any SSE subscriber -> a distance proxy, without an RSSI field ever being on the wire. Age-out churn leaks the same way.

Fix: emit every tracked aircraft on a fixed cadence (~1 Hz), holding last-known position, regardless of how many raw messages actually arrived. Near aircraft down-sampled, quiet ones up-sampled by holding. Uniform TTL, uniform drop behaviour.

Cost: gives up the sub-second freshness adsb-jpf just bought, for close aircraft specifically. That is the trade -- 'instant for the ones near me, laggy for the ones far away' IS the signal.

SEVERITY IS CONDITIONAL and lower than it first looks: with a crop that sits strictly inside uniform coverage, the residual is a weak monotone gradient swamped by transponder power variance, aircraft antenna nulls, multipath, and our own bounded-queue drops. Do the coverage-polygon bead FIRST -- it determines whether this one matters much or barely at all.

title: Jump-flagging (position-suspect) does not ride the upsert path
description: Scope boundary left by adsb-jpf (noted in its report): the spoof-fingerprint jump check (adsb.ingest.plausibility -> :aircraft/position-suspect?) applies on the batch/snapshot path only. Per-delta upserts are range-gated (same gate-range as the batch path, wired in main.clj) but NOT jump-flagged, so an implausible position jump streams to clients unflagged until the client next renders a snapshot-derived state. Decide where the jump check belongs on the delta path (the accumulator sees previous vs next state and already owns the merge; the hook in tcp/accumulate! sees the merged aircraft) and apply it so upserts carry the same trust marks as batches. The flag is surfaced-never-dropped semantics — keep that.
acceptance_criteria: A position jump arriving via a streaming delta sets :aircraft/position-suspect? on the upsert a client receives; consistent observation clears it; batch path behavior unchanged; tests cover the delta path; bb test and lint pass.

title: Remove dead reckoning: the map renders the latest real position only
description:
Decision (owner, 2026-07-14): no predicted motion. The per-second stutter — planes gliding forward on projection then snapping back on the next real frame — existed under polling too and is inherent to dead reckoning against a ~1 Hz feed whose 'last heard' stamp advances independently of the position (REPL-verified: a velocity-only message re-stamps seen-at-ms and yanks a 450 kt projection ~185 m backward). Rather than adding a position-at-ms clock to make projection honest, remove projection: an aircraft renders at its last real position until a new one arrives, hopping at feed cadence.

Scope: adsb.geo/projectable? and project-aircraft (and their tests) go away; adsb.map.aircraft-layer stops dead-reckoning its cached picture and pushes real positions only. CAREFUL: the layer's render loop also drives the staleness fade (age-property/stale flag against wall clock) — that behavior must survive, whether the loop stays for fade alone or the fade is re-derived some other way. seen-at-ms semantics (age-out, fade) are unchanged everywhere.
acceptance_criteria: No projection code remains (geo or layer); aircraft render at their last real position, updating at feed cadence; staleness fade and stale flag behave exactly as before against wall clock; no other consumer of project-aircraft survives (grep); full bb test (clj + browser cljs) and bb lint pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-ICAO delta accumulator: merge per-message field deltas into aircraft state (pure cljc)
description:
Both TCP Sources (SBS adsb-0in, Beast adsb-doh) deliver per-message field deltas — a callsign here, a position there, a velocity there — where aircraft.json delivered complete snapshots. Something must merge those deltas into per-ICAO aircraft state and age silent entries out. That merge is pure logic and belongs in src/cljc/: (message-delta, state, now-ms) -> state, with time as an argument, no atoms, no clock — same rules as the rest of the shared domain.

Deltas arrive as partial :aircraft/* maps (each Source has already coerced its wire format at its own boundary before handing deltas in — this namespace never sees SBS or Beast shapes). Reuse the existing age-out (adsb.aircraft/aged-out?) rather than inventing a second staleness rule. The accumulator owns nothing stateful itself; the Source that uses it holds the atom.

This lands before either Source: it is the shared substance of both, and testing it as pure domain (literal maps in, literal now-ms) beats testing it through a socket.
acceptance_criteria: A pure cljc namespace merges partial :aircraft/* delta maps into per-ICAO state; later fields win, absent fields persist; silent aircraft age out via the existing threshold; no I/O, atoms, or clock reads; property-style tests cover interleaved deltas and age-out; bb test and bb lint pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: SBS Source: stream ultrafeeder's port 30003 instead of polling aircraft.json
description:
Implement an adsb.ingest.source/Source over ultrafeeder's SBS BaseStation feed (TCP dietpi.local:30003). SBS is plain-text CSV — one line per Mode-S message — so it gets us a socket held open across polls and lower latency than aircraft.json polling, without decoding raw frames. That's why it goes first; Beast (binary, port 30005) follows as its own Source (adsb-doh).

Shape: open! connects and starts a reader that feeds parsed messages through the shared per-ICAO delta accumulator (adsb-0qa) into state this Source owns; fetch! returns the current coerced domain batch; close! tears the socket down. The Source is the trust boundary for the SBS wire format (docs/validation-boundaries.md): parse defensively, coerce each line to a partial :aircraft/* delta at this boundary, and nothing SBS-shaped may leak past it — the accumulator, poll loop, and domain stay oblivious (src/clj/adsb/ingest/source.clj).

Never test against the live feeder — record an SBS session and replay it (the existing fixture-replay pattern, adsb-nqf.4).
acceptance_criteria: An SBS Source implements open!/fetch!/close! (and Metadata if the feed offers payload-level facts); fetch! returns coerced, validated domain aircraft; malformed/hostile SBS lines are dropped at the boundary with tests proving it; a recorded SBS fixture drives the tests; swapping Sources requires no change outside ingest wiring; bb test and bb lint pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: Desktop ruler affordances: hover names nearest tick, zoom readout + reset
description: Scoped down after reading the suite: an-idle-pointer-over-the-ruler-does-not-scrub deliberately encodes that an unpressed mouse must not hijack the hover — the ticks' 9px hit-slop is the desktop naming channel, and it works once the hover lifecycle (adsb-o7n) is fixed. What remains: wheel/pinch zoom is an invisible affordance with no on-screen state and no way back but an undocumented double-click. Add a ZOOM census chip (same idiom as GND/NO ALT/EMG/PLOTTED) that appears while zoom > 1, states the ratio, and resets to the whole sky on click; crosshair cursor on the ruler view.
close_reason: ZOOM census chip while zoom > 1 (states x-ratio, resets on press), crosshair cursor on the ruler view. Reset is one dispatch — the old rAF read-back raced the render (verified live: full ruler wearing a zoomed window's overflow counts) and is gone. Verified live on desktop and at 390px headless: chip joins the caption line, reset restores full window with zero overflow markers. Commit ce3ff39.

title: Drawer wears the index card's face on desktop; flush corner stays phone-only
description: The one drawer is fixed flush to the top-left corner in both stances. Right on a phone (edge sheets are the native idiom), wrong on desktop where it reads as a strip stuck in the corner, unrelated to the detail panel it shares the screen with. Fix: extract the index-card surface (paper-veil, rule-strong border, 2px radius, offset shadow, settle animation) into a shared decl both the panel and the drawer compose; desktop drawer floats inset var(--s3); phone keeps the flush corner. Also fix the drawer max-height subtracting --stack-w from 100vh on desktop — a phone-stance assumption (Stack at bottom) applied where the Stack is on the right.
close_reason: Second pass: adsb.css.card now owns the head (ink rule, baseline row, s3/s3/s2 padding) and the title stamp (t1 bold tracked) as well as the face and close; panel and drawer both compose them. Verified side by side at 1440 — the two cards print identically except for content. Commit b64484f.

title: Delete the corner legend; the Stack is the map key
description:
The legend printed an ALTITUDE table of five swatches — a worse copy of the ruler, which §9 already declares is its own legend (its fill IS the altitude gradient, built from the same style/palette the planes are painted from, with flight levels ruled across it). And it printed a STATUS table whose rows were already said elsewhere, or said nothing at all.

Deleted, and the map key moves onto the Stack:

  * ALTITUDE — the ruler. A continuous ramp with graduations is not a worse legend than five discrete swatches; it is a better one, and it was already on the chart.
  * ON GROUND / NO ALTITUDE — the shelf counts now lead with a SWATCH: '● GND 3', '● NO ALT 31'. A legend row and a count in one breath. The swatch earns its place in the two spots the dot cluster cannot help: on a phone, where the dots are hidden, and at a count of zero, where there are no dots to take the colour from.
  * EMERGENCY — '● EMG 1', rendered ONLY while an aircraft is squawking distress. A legend row explaining a colour that is nowhere on the chart spends the reader's attention on nothing. This one is on screen exactly when red is.
  * STALE — deleted. The fade keys itself: a plane going faint is a plane going quiet, and the panel says SEEN 7s ago.

THE INVARIANT THE LEGEND WAS REALLY PROTECTING IS KEPT. Its one true virtue was never the box — it was that it built every swatch from style/palette directly, so a re-skin moved the key and the planes together and they could not drift. Had the key moved onto the Stack as CSS tokens mirroring the palette, that guarantee would have died quietly with the box. The swatches are painted inline from the palette, and the test asserts they equal it IN BOTH EDITIONS — the deleted legend test, ported.

With the stats already in the header (adsb-33i), nothing was left in the margin column but the box, so the column, its namespace, and its stylesheet go too.
close_reason:
Legend deleted; the Stack is the key. Verified in the running app at 390px and 1440px: no .adsb-legend, no .adsb-margin, and the swatches render the palette exactly (GND #8A8374, NO ALT #9A937F — the day edition's own ground/unknown inks, read back off data-color and matching the painted background).

The margin column is gone entirely — component, stylesheet namespace, and the div. The bottom-left corner is map again. app.css: 21,764 -> 20,706 bytes.

THE ONE THING THAT NEEDED CARE, recorded for whoever revisits this: the legend's value was not the box, it was that it built its swatches from style/palette directly, so the key could not drift from the planes across a re-skin. Moving the key onto the Stack as CSS tokens would have silently killed that. The swatches are painted inline from the palette instead, and legend_test's both-editions equality assertion is ported onto them (the-key-cannot-drift-from-the-map-in-either-edition), including the not= guard proving the two editions really are different inks so the test is a live constraint and not a tautology.

docs/design-direction.md §11 amended: the margin column is recorded as deleted, with the three moves that emptied it (adsb-33i stats to the header, adsb-sod key to the Stack, adsb-5c7 the credit folded).

884 assertions green (the 2 reds are adsb-kc4).

title: Fold the map attribution to its (i) button by default
description:
MapLibre's compact attribution control is compact and OPEN: it sets maplibregl-compact (so the (i) button exists) AND maplibregl-compact-show (so it is expanded anyway), collapsing only on the reader's first interaction with the map. So every session opened with a banner of running text across the map's bottom edge — 8,453px2 on a phone, and 16,280px2 when it wrapped to two lines. It was the widest chrome we had, and the only piece that said the same thing every time.

Folded to its (i): 576px2. The margin column also drops back down — it had been lifted 56px purely to clear the wrapped banner.

NOT HIDDEN. The credit is required (OpenFreeMap / OpenMapTiles / OpenStreetMap ride the style JSON's sources) and it is not removed, not disabled, and not unreachable: the (i) button is MapLibre's own first-class mode for exactly this, and one tap opens the full text. If MapLibre ever renames the classes, the fold quietly does nothing and the credit simply stays open — the failure mode leaves it visible, never missing.
close_reason:
Corrected and verified. The credit now SHOWS, then folds after five seconds — the timeout the OSMF guidelines name by number — with MapLibre's own collapse-on-pan/zoom still firing first if the reader touches the map. Both are permitted triggers; the (i) is reachable forever after either way.

attribution-fold-ms is a licence term wearing a number's clothes. Its docstring and its test both say so: it may not go to zero, and it may not be shortened. If someone later 'optimises' it to fold on load, they will break the licence, and the test will stop them.

Verified in the running app at 390px: banner shown at t=2.5s (352x24), folded at t=6.5s (24x24), and folded at t=2.4s when panned early. The margin column still reclaims its 56px of map — it just waits for the fold, stepping down when the map container gains the .adsb-credit-folded mark.

LESSON, recorded because it nearly shipped: I asserted 'the credit is one tap away, so this is fine' without reading the guidelines. The guidelines were one WebFetch away and they disagreed. A compaction that touches a REQUIRED attribution is a licensing question first and a design question second.

title: The header reports exceptions, not confirmations — dot-only feeder, silent-when-live stream, stats moved up
description:
Three asks from the design conversation, and they turn out to be one rule.

TODAY the header spends its width telling you that nothing is wrong: a chip reading
'Live' (which it is, essentially always) and a chip reading 'Feeder OK' (likewise). And
the two session scalars — MAX RANGE, MSG RATE — sit in a bordered chip in the margin
column, covering the map, boxed for no reason now that a box contains only two numbers.

THE RULE: chrome reports EXCEPTIONS, not confirmations. Silence means healthy.

  * STREAM chip — renders nothing while :live. Reconnecting and Disconnected still
    shout. The signal is kept; only the confirmation is dropped. ('A frozen map must
    not read as a quiet one' — still true, and still honoured: a frozen map is not a
    silent header, it is a Disconnected chip.)

  * FEEDER chip — a coloured dot alone when :ok. Any NON-ok state (starting, down,
    unknown) keeps its dot AND its words.

    This is deliberately NOT 'the feeder is just a dot'. The header's own docstring
    records the decision that colour alone is not accessible, and it is right: a
    colour-blind user must not be the only one who cannot see that the antenna is
    dead. But that rule bites on PROBLEMS, not on the benign state — a green dot
    saying 'nothing to see here' is fine, provided the a11y tree still carries the
    words (a visually-hidden label) and provided every problem state speaks. So: the
    dot alone is allowed to mean OK, and is never allowed to mean DOWN.

  * MAX RANGE / MSG RATE — up into the header, where the other session vitals (the
    counts) already live, unboxed, in the caption voice they already print in. One
    home, both stances, no new stance-specific geometry. The margin column keeps only
    the legend, and stops covering as much map.

The room for the scalars is precisely the room the first two changes free. That is why
this is one bead and not three.

DONE WHEN
- Live stream renders no stream chip; reconnecting/down do.
- Feeder ok is a bare dot with a visually-hidden label; every other state shows words.
- Max range and msg rate read from the header in both stances; the stats chip's box is
  gone from the margin column.
- Browser tests cover each. bb lint, bb test green.
close_reason:
Done, and verified in the running app at 390px and 1440px — both stances fit with room to spare.

Stream chip: absent while live. Reconnecting/Disconnected keep everything they had.
Feeder: a bare 9px green dot at :ok, no box, label moved to the a11y tree via a new
.adsb-vh utility; starting/down/unknown keep their words in plain sight.
Stats: MAX RANGE and MSG RATE now read from the header beside the counts, unboxed; the
margin column is down to the legend alone.

Phone header now reads: adsb | 75 · 65 | MAX RANGE 256 km | MSG RATE 337/s | ●

Tests: a live stream renders no chip and no word; a healthy feeder's label is present in
the DOM but carries .adsb-vh; every problem state's label does NOT; the scalars read from
inside the header with their units. 881 assertions green (the 2 failures are adsb-kc4).

FOLLOW-UP WORTH A LOOK: the margin column's legend still prints an ALTITUDE colour ramp —
which is precisely what the Stack's ruler IS (design-direction §9: 'the ruler's fill is the
altitude gradient — it is its own legend'). That box is now the largest piece of chrome
covering the map, and half of it is a second copy of the ruler. Filing separately if wanted.

title: adsb.stream-test/backoff-grows fails deterministically in the browser suite
description:
bb test:cljs fails 2 assertions in adsb.stream-test/backoff-grows (adsb/stream_test.cljs:180,182): !delays is empty, so no reconnect was ever scheduled.

  expected: [1000 2000 4000 8000]
  actual:   []

NOT flaky (3/3 runs) and NOT caused by the Garden CSS work (adsb-chh): reproduced at commit 0814925 in a clean worktree, before any of those changes. Distinct from adsb-a07 (a flaky JVM run).

Either the exponential-backoff reconnect regressed, or the test no longer drives the CLOSED path it thinks it does. The suite is red on master either way.
close_reason: Fixed in 9572326: reg-fx now wraps schedule-reconnect! in a lambda so the test's redef seam actually works. backoff-grows passes; full cljs suite green (182 tests, 898 assertions).

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE: neither hypothesis in the description. The backoff did not regress, and the test does drive the CLOSED path.

The seam the test redefs never existed. src/cljs/adsb/stream.cljs registered the effect by passing the fn VALUE:

    (rf/reg-fx :stream/schedule-reconnect! schedule-reconnect!)

reg-fx captures whatever object it is handed, so it held the ORIGINAL fn forever. with-redefs rebinds the var, which the effect handler never consults again — the test's recording fake sat unused while the real js/setTimeout was armed. !delays stayed empty because nothing ever called it. Its two siblings (:stream/connect!, :stream/clear-timer!) wrap the call in a lambda, which is why they alone honored their redefs.

FIX: wrap the call, matching the siblings — (fn [ms] (schedule-reconnect! ms)). Landed in 9572326.

PRODUCTION WAS NEVER BROKEN. reg-fx held the real schedule-reconnect!, so live reconnect always armed a real timer on the correct exponential backoff. This was purely a test-observability defect: the assertion could not see what the app was correctly doing.

WIDER FALLOUT: every test that redefined schedule-reconnect! merely to AVOID arming a timer (connection-state-tracks-reality, update-replaces-wholesale) was in fact arming real ones and passing anyway.

NEVER PASSED. The test and the value-passed reg-fx landed in the same commit, 2318c22 (2026-07-11 21:02). The browser runner predates it by an hour (f7a46a1, 19:58), so this suite has been red on master since the feature was written — it was not a later regression.

title: Flaky JVM test run: one bb test:clj failed, not reproducible in 8 subsequent runs
description:
Seen once on 2026-07-12, on the committed state of adsb-kh4.7 (HEAD 567a101). 'bb test:clj' exited nonzero; the Clojure error report confirmed REAL failures ('Test failures or errors occurred.' from cognitect.test-runner.api/test), not a harness glitch. The failing test NAMES were not captured — the output was piped through tail and scrolled. Eight subsequent runs (5 plain, 1 under heavy CPU load, 2 ad hoc) were all green: 146 tests / 630 assertions, 0 failures.

So: a real, intermittent failure with an unknown trigger. Do not close this by running the suite once and seeing green.

PRIME SUSPECT — shared mutable global across test namespaces. adsb.http.server holds the server in a defonce atom and start! is IDEMPOTENT: it returns the EXISTING server if one is up rather than starting a new one. THREE namespaces drive that one global — adsb.http.server-test, adsb.stream.broadcast-test (start-streaming-server!), and adsb.http.security-test (headers-survive-a-real-http-response). If any of them leaves a server up when the next starts, that next start! silently attaches to the PREVIOUS namespace's handler and dependencies, and (http-kit/server-port srv) then names the wrong server. http-kit's stop is also asynchronous while the atom is reset synchronously, so 'stopped' and 'gone' are not the same instant.

SECOND SUSPECT — broadcast-test asserts on millisecond SSE timing (interval-ms, heartbeat-ms) against a real socket; the failure happened during a burst of git commits, i.e. under load. But the suite stayed green under deliberate CPU saturation, which weakens this.

TO INVESTIGATE: run the suite in a loop capturing FULL output until it fails (do not pipe through tail), then read the failing test name. Consider making the tests not share the defonce server — an explicit start-on-ephemeral-port returning a handle, rather than a global that start! will hand back to whoever asks next.
acceptance_criteria: The failing test is identified from a captured full-output run, the mechanism explained, and the shared-server-atom hazard either fixed or ruled out with evidence. Not closed on a single green run.
close_reason: Two real races fixed in faa8cea: start! was idempotent and handed callers someone else's server (silently discarding their options), and poll/stop! never joined the loop thread, so a dying poller could refill a picture a test had just cleared. The 2026-07-12 failure itself was never reproduced, so this is not proven to be THE cause — but both bugs produce its exact signature and both are closed. The millisecond SSE timing assertion (second suspect) lives on as adsb-hr3. Reopen if it recurs.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
INVESTIGATION 2026-07-14.

RULED OUT, with evidence:

1. Shadow-rebuild race on the static bundle. routes-test/static-serving asserts 200 for
   /js/main.js, which is gitignored BUILD OUTPUT that the `bb dev` shadow watch rewrites
   (7.3 MB). Looked like a strong candidate. It is not: shadow writes atomically. Polled
   main.js 1986 times across a real rebuild (touch a .cljs, watch it recompile) — never
   absent, never truncated. Proved separately that ABSENCE does 404 it (18/400 requests
   during a delete/recreate cycle), so the test IS coupled to build output — but nothing
   in normal operation removes the file. A fresh clone / CI without a build fails it
   deterministically, not intermittently.

2. Namespace ordering. The runner runs namespaces alphabetically and deterministically.
   A pure order dependency would fail EVERY run, not one in nine. So a07 must be a
   genuine thread race, not an order bug. This narrowed the search a lot.

3. The original "green under heavy CPU load" control was INVALID. Found 12 orphaned `yes`
   processes (PPID 1) burning ~8 cores since 2026-07-12 — the abandoned load generator
   from this bug's own first investigation. Killed them; load average went 178 -> 10.
   Every measurement since, including that original control, was taken on a machine
   already at saturation.

MECHANISM CONFIRMED AND DEMONSTRATED (prime suspect was right, and worse than filed):

start! was idempotent via (or @server ...), so it returned the RUNNING server and silently
DISCARDED the options it was passed. Demonstrated against the pre-fix code: caller A starts
a plain server; caller B asks for one with :stream-connect on its own port; B is handed A's
server (identical? true, same port, only ONE "listening on port" logged), and GET /api/stream
on it returns 503 instead of streaming. A test that leaked would silently exercise the
previous namespace's handler and fail in a way that reads as nonsense.

The bead listed three namespaces on that atom. There were FOUR — adsb.main (main.clj:323)
drives it too, which is how a test booting the real system could collide with the http tests.

SECOND RACE, not in the original report: poll/stop! interrupted the loop thread but never
JOINED it. The loop calls on-batch!, which in the assembled system writes to adsb.state. So
main-test's (main/stop! system) followed by (state/clear!) could be followed in turn by the
still-dying poller repopulating the global picture it had just cleared.

FIXES (each with a regression test that is RED against the pre-fix code):
- server/start-server! + stop-server!: every caller owns its handle; nothing shared. The
  defonce global remains, but ONLY as the REPL's convenience, and only its own test drives
  it now. stop-server! derefs http-kit's stop promise, so "stopped" and "gone" are one
  instant.
- poll/stop! joins the loop thread (bounded, 5 s) — no batch can land after it returns.
- adsb.main owns its server handle instead of the global.

NOT REPRODUCED. I did not catch the original failure. One apparent repro early on turned out
to be a concurrent agent's half-finished edit to tcp.clj — discarded, not this bug. Both races
above are real and now closed, but I cannot claim either IS the 2026-07-12 failure, only that
both could produce exactly its signature: real assertion failures, intermittent, gone on replay.

Also filed adsb-hr3: broadcast-test's (< elapsed-ms 100) wall-clock assertion is a standing
flake risk — but it landed 2026-07-13, a day AFTER a07, so it is not the cause.

title: Clear out the preview fitting room (#/preview) after the §5 picks
description: Overseer: 'clear out the previews.' The pick is made and applied; remove the preview route/page/stylesheet/tests, revert the router, drop the audition-only fonts (Source Serif 4, IBM Plex Mono, Inter), amend §5's 'the preview remains' line. Shipped faces (Space Mono, Space Grotesk) stay.
close_reason: Cleared in the commit above: preview page/stylesheet/tests deleted, adsb.core reverted to the plain shell mount (stale #/preview links just show the chart — verified live), index.html link removed, audition-only fonts (Source Serif 4, IBM Plex Mono, Inter) and their LICENSE sections dropped; shipped faces (Space Mono, Space Grotesk) untouched. §5 mechanism note updated to point future re-skins at git history. kh4.7 CSP note refreshed (faces + @font-face now in app.css). Suite green after removal (155 tests/778 assertions, lint clean); app bundle 152->134 modules.

title: Stage caption-voice options in #/preview: scribbled vs printed, new label faces
description: Overseer request during the dgb.10 endgame (Annotation crowned): the detail panel's italic Title Case captions strain at small sizes; upper-case sans reads far easier. Stage a sixth preview dimension — caption voice — with: scribbled (italic mono Title Case, C as drawn), printed mono caps, printed Space Grotesk caps (new OFL face, Space Mono's proportional sibling), printed Inter caps (new OFL face). Self-hosted woff2s + LICENSE additions. Outcome feeds §5/dgb.5.
close_reason: Staged in commit (see git log): #/preview gains a Captions dimension — scribbled / printed-mono / printed-grotesk / printed-inter — orthogonal to typography, persisted in the URL like the rest. Space Grotesk 500 + Inter 600 OFL woff2s self-hosted with LICENSE notes. Verified live: all four voices compute correctly on the crowned mix (10.5px italic vs 8.5px caps) and the new faces load. Suite green (166 browser tests/871 assertions, lint clean). Overseer picks the caption voice; the pick amends design-direction §5's caption clause and dgb.5 applies it.

title: Emergency map annotations: red-pen double ellipse, MAYDAY stamp, off-screen edge arrow (§7)
description: The three §7 map-surface treatments the visual pass (adsb-dgb.5) deliberately did NOT half-ship: (1) the hand-drawn red-pen DOUBLE ELLIPSE circling an emergency aircraft — draws itself once and stays, ink never blinks; (2) the rotated MAYDAY + squawk STAMP beside it carrying callsign, altitude, vertical rate; (3) the off-screen EDGE ARROW at the map boundary pointing toward an emergency the viewport cannot see (Q13c — the camera is never hijacked). Today the map surface carries the emergency via color/size override (style.cljs) plus the NOTAM strip, hatched Stack tick, and panel badge — unmissable in chrome, but the CHART itself is not yet annotated. Implementation notes: the compass-pencil selection ring (adsb.map.selection, adsb-dgb.5) established the pattern — a low-churn MapLibre marker carrying SVG, driven by a track! outside React, marker seam in adsb.map.maplibre (add-marker!/move-marker!/remove-marker!). Emergencies are rare and few, so markers are the right tool; the ellipse and stamp are one marker per emergency icao, the edge arrow needs map bounds (extend the seam with a bounds/on-move accessor). Zero animation on any of it beyond the one-time draw-in of the ellipse (§6/§7: emergencies spend zero motion).
close_reason: Shipped: §7 map annotations complete. Red-pen double ellipse (draws in once — iteration-count 1 provable inline — then holds), MAYDAY stamp (shared emergency-words copy, data-only 1 Hz updates, track-perpendicular placement), edge arrow (pure adsb.geo/edge-annotation for edge point + great-circle bearing + distance, chrome-aware pixel insets, click fires existing select — camera never hijacked). Seam grew bounds/on-move!. bb test 842 assertions green, lint clean; verified live both editions + off-screen, screenshots in session scratchpad emergency-annotations/. Commit 21ae3f0. NOTE: concurrent adsb.stream backoff-grows failure is in-flight backend WIP, not this bead.

title: Implement perspective-size instinct-altitude; delete cast-shadow layer
description: Overseer pick from adsb-dgb.12: icon-size becomes the instinct-altitude channel (low=near=large 1.25 at deck -> 0.55 at FL400; emergency override kept; mlat demotion becomes a multiplicative factor so it composes with the altitude size instead of colliding). Cast-shadow layer deleted entirely per the pick (geo.cljc shadow-offset math+property, style.cljs shadow inks/expressions/spec/toggle, aircraft_layer soft icons+wiring, all shadow tests). Tether not implemented. Preview rig (uncommitted) removed.
close_reason: Perspective size implemented (commit 581befa): icon-size is the instinct-altitude channel — perspective-size-stops [[0 1.25][10000 1.0][40000 0.55]], front-loaded so the low sky keeps resolution; emergency 1.6 overrides absolutely; mlat demotion now a multiplicative 0.78 factor composed with the altitude size; ground 0.5 deliberately beneath the whole flying ramp (apron clutter defers — a size-test invariant caught the original 0.7 contradicting the declared intent, and the constant obeyed the intent). Cast shadow DELETED per the pick, not toggled: geo shadow-offset math+property, shadow inks/expressions/spec/soft-SDF variants, all shadow tests; preview rig removed (was never committed). Verified in the running app at replay density, both editions (scratchpad altitude-instinct/final-perspective-{day,night}.png): high cruisers recede, low traffic leans forward, night identical to day because geometry carries no ink. bb test 609+834 green, bb lint clean.

title: Explore instinct-altitude alternatives (cast shadow rejected)
description: Overseer verdict 2026-07-12 on the shadow prototype: too busy at traffic density and nearly invisible in dark mode - rejected, toggle now off. The Q6 desire stands: altitude should have an instinct channel beyond the color ramp. Explore alternatives as cheap visual proofs (scratchpad mockups or throwaway branches of the running app, both editions, real density): candidates might include icon size scaling with altitude, a short vertical drop-line tether, halo weight, label superscript flight levels, or leaning harder on the Stack as the sole profile channel and deleting the shadow layer. Deliver evidence, not opinions; the Overseer picks. The shadow layer's fate (delete vs replace) resolves with the pick.
acceptance_criteria: 2-4 alternatives demonstrated with day+night screenshots at replay density; an honest recommendation with tradeoffs; Overseer pick recorded; follow-up implementation bead filed for the winner (or the channel is declared closed).
notes: OVERSEER PICK RECORDED (2026-07-12, direct): PERSPECTIVE SIZE. Tether and shadow both rejected ('I don't like the tether or the shadow'). Resolution: implement altitude-perspective icon sizing; DELETE the shadow layer code (geo + style + layer + tests); remove the uncommitted preview rig. Implementation bead to follow.
close_reason: Overseer picked PERSPECTIVE SIZE from the four proofs (baseline/size/tether/size+tether; halo-weight disqualified on SDF physics); tether and shadow explicitly rejected by the Overseer. Evidence in scratchpad altitude-instinct/ + README; live ?preview= rig demonstrated all candidates in the running app and was removed after the pick. Winner implemented and shipped as adsb-b23 (commit 581befa) — shadow layer deleted with it, resolving adsb-dgb.8's remnant. Channel settled: colour = precision, size = instinct, Stack = profile.

title: In-app /preview pages for typography, scale, spacing, and palette options
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-12: the type/scale/palette pick will be made from IN-APP preview pages, not static screenshots. Build a client-side preview route (hash-route like #/preview - NO backend changes; the backend is an active Overseer construction zone for the App Platform pivot) rendering real chrome samples (header, detail panel, legend, Stack excerpt, alert ribbon) under switchable options across four dimensions: typography systems (start from the three bake-off candidates: Marginalia / Drafting Room / The Annotation), modular scales, spacing scales, and color palette variants - each dimension toggleable independently, in both day and night editions. Self-hosted OFL fonts only (CSP: font-src 'self' will be needed - coordinate with the header migration in adsb.http.security, do not edit it yourself; note it instead). The Overseer browses, mixes, and picks; the pick then closes adsb-dgb.10 and feeds the visual pass.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev serves #/preview; all four dimensions independently switchable against real chrome samples in both editions; bake-off candidates present among the typography options; no backend files touched; suite green.
close_reason: Re-closed after Overseer-found caption defect fixed in 719645e (original ship 817b94f): the preview had generalized Drafting Room's --t-2 caption size to all three systems, rendering Marginalia's and The Annotation's captions a token smaller than their bake-off specs. Captions now default to --t-1 with B (and C's small pen headings) carrying their own --t-2 — each candidate judged as designed. Verified on the Overseer's own preferred mix (annotation/major-13/airy-4/wine-pen, both editions): fact labels compute 10.5px under Annotation@major-13, 8.5px caps under Drafting Room (its spec), 10px under Marginalia. CSS-only change; suite untouched and green from 817b94f. adsb-dgb.10 still open for the final pick.

title: Static asset compression on App Platform
description: Caddy gzip/zstd-compresses the ~1MB compiled frontend (encode directive, restricted to a type allowlist so it never touches text/event-stream). App Platform has no Caddy, and whether DO's edge compresses a service response is unverified. If it does not, every first load ships ~1MB of uncompressed JS. Deliberately NOT hand-rolled during adsb-9n6: a compression middleware that catches text/event-stream would buffer the SSE stream and kill the map, and doing it safely means either caching the compressed static set or excluding the stream by content-type — worth doing properly, not in passing. STEP 1 is to measure: curl -H 'Accept-Encoding: gzip' against the deployed app and read Content-Encoding.

title: Typography, scale and palette bake-off
description: Q14 of the design questionnaire was deliberately DEFERRED, not answered. The Overseer will not settle type in the abstract: this decision sets the chrome for the whole site and he wants to see it, not guess at it. Once a real layout exists (map + the Stack), stage a side-by-side bake-off of competing typefaces, modular scales, spacing scales, and color palettes against the real thing.
design: Sectional's drawn leaning was editorial — serif headers, italic captions, mono data blocks — but that is a starting hypothesis for the bake-off, NOT the decision. Blocked in spirit (not by dependency) on the Stack and the night edition existing, since there is nothing to bake off against until then.
acceptance_criteria: The Overseer picks a type system, a modular scale, a spacing scale, and a palette by looking at real candidates side by side; the winner is written into docs/design-direction.md.
notes:
BAKE-OFF STAGED — awaiting Overseer pick (bead deliberately left open; winner gets written into design-direction.md §5 and applied in adsb-dgb.5).

Three complete type systems staged against the REAL app (map+Stack+header+legend+panel, replay traffic, both editions) via runtime CSS injection in headless Chromium (Playwright addStyleTag, bypassCSP) — zero repo edits per candidate. 12 screenshots, identical framings (full day/night + detail-panel/Stack close-up day/night, DAL1275 selected), all verified captured while the SSE chip read Live.

CANDIDATES:
A. MARGINALIA (the §5 editorial hypothesis, done properly): Source Serif 4 display/body + SS4 Italic captions + IBM Plex Mono data. Scale 1.333 base 13 (10/13/17/23). Spacing 4px airy, header 48px. All OFL.
B. DRAFTING ROOM (mid-century engineering drawing): Futura Medium caps tracked 0.18-0.22em display (ship Jost OFL as stand-in) + Avenir Next labels (ship Nunito Sans/Inter OFL) + IBM Plex Mono data. Scale 1.2 base 12. Strict 8px grid, radius 0, double-ruled frames, header 40px.
C. THE ANNOTATION (pure mono): Space Mono only (bold/italic/regular; OFL) — hierarchy by weight/case, magenta promoted to the plotter's-pen accent (title underline, legend headings). Scale 1.2 base 12, shallow by design.

Artifacts (session scratchpad): bake-off/README.md (specs+arguments+shots), bake-off/shots/*.png (12), bake-off/candidate-*.css + shared-scaffold.css (layout-only dressing for the still-unstyled panel/stats/feeder-chip placeholders, identical under all candidates), shoot.mjs (repeatable rig), fonts/*.woff2 (OFL).

FINDING for the winner's implementation: app CSP has default-src 'none' and NO font-src — self-hosted fonts need font-src 'self' added in adsb.http.security. Auteur's advisory (not a decision): A/Marginalia — most in The Sectional's voice; B reads sterner than the direction, C is charming but shallow-scaled at density.
close_reason: PICKED by the Overseer 2026-07-12, live in #/preview (adsb-dgb.11): typography=annotation scale=major-13 spacing=compact-4 palette=wine-pen — The Annotation (Space Mono only, weight/case hierarchy, magenta pen), major third 1.25@13px (tokens 8.5/10.5/13/16/20), compact 4px spacing (2/4/8/12/16, header 36px), wine-pen accents (magenta #A83A63/#E77E9B, chrome aero #36547E/#8BA9D6). Written into docs/design-direction.md §5 (+ §2 accent rows re-inked with provenance note) in commit 03efeb7. Scale note: Overseer confirmed Annotation and compact explicitly; major-13 accepted via Auteur's advisory (1.333@13 was his other finalist — a one-token flip in §5 if he reverses). Applied to the real chrome by the visual pass adsb-dgb.5; CSP font-src via adsb-kh4.7. Basemap place-label voice deliberately left to dgb.5's eye.

title: Build 'the Stack' — the live altitude ruler
description: Commissioned in the design questionnaire (Q8): there is NO aircraft-list sidebar. The Overseer rejected all three sidebar options as 'a table in a costume'. The replacement is the Stack — a vertical flight-level ruler down one edge of the map where every aircraft is a live tick mark at its TRUE altitude, drifting up and down as it climbs or descends. It is a profile view of the sky beside the plan view of it, and it does three jobs at once: altitude legend (its fill IS the Q6 gradient), altitude scale, and aircraft list. Hover a tick to light that aircraft on the map; click to select. Traffic stacking on approach and a departure climbing out become visible as MOTION on the ruler rather than rows re-sorting in a table.
design: On phone the ruler lies down: a horizontal strip along an edge, identical semantics (Q9c — neither stage may degrade). This is chrome, so it IS Reagent/re-frame — but it updates at feeder cadence with every aircraft in it, so watch the render cost; it may need the same escape hatch the aircraft layer uses. Open question: what happens when 40 aircraft occupy the same 2000ft? Jitter, cluster, or fan out. Related: the rejected ATC flight-progress strip rack may return as the SELECTION detail surface.
acceptance_criteria: The Stack replaces the sidebar entirely, works on phone and desktop, and a user can find and select any aircraft through it without a list ever appearing.
notes: SUPERSEDES the already-built sidebar (adsb-dgb.2, closed). That sidebar was shipped before the design direction was settled; Q8 of the questionnaire rejects it outright ('a table in a costume'). When the Stack lands, the sidebar comes OUT — this bead owns its removal, including its re-frame subs/events and any layout that assumes a two-column desktop stage. Do not leave both in the app 'for now'.
close_reason: The Stack is live and the sidebar is gone (commit 58ba374). adsb.ui.stack: a flight-level ruler (surface–FL450) on the right edge, lying along the bottom edge on phone — one DOM, --alt-pct mapped to bottom/left, neither stage degraded. The fill is built from adsb.map.style/palettes per edition, so the ruler IS the legend and cannot drift. Pure layout fns (altitude-pct, tick-band, stack-member?, ruler-gradient-css) unit-tested as arithmetic; airborne ticks at true proportional altitude with a sub-cadence CSS drift, ground cluster shelf, altitude-absent holding shelf (absent is never zero). Hover publishes :aircraft/hovered-icao (map-side highlight is a recorded follow-up), click fires the existing [:aircraft/select] contract, selected tick magenta+named, emergency tick red/hatched/always-named — nothing blinks. Verified visually against the replay fixture in headless Chromium: 44 ticks spanning 1.6–88.9%, approach stacking visible as overlapping ticks at FL330. Acceptance met: any aircraft findable and selectable with no list anywhere. bb test 150/742 green, bb lint clean.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: FOLLOW-UPS recorded at close: (1) MAP-SIDE HOVER HIGHLIGHT — the Stack publishes :aircraft/hovered-icao (events :aircraft/hover / :aircraft/clear-hover in adsb.events, sub in adsb.subs) and the tick shows its own hover affordance, but the map layer does not yet light the hovered aircraft (feature-state or highlight property), nor does hovering a plane light its tick; wire both in a later map wave. (2) FILTERS — dgb.2's sort/filter capability retired with the sidebar; filters may return INSIDE the Stack later if missed. (3) RENDER COST — measured with the 44-tick replay sky: per-second Reagent re-render is noise; if a much busier sky gets heavy, memoize the per-aircraft tick (noted in adsb.ui.stack docstring).

title: Prototype altitude-as-cast-shadow
description: Commissioned in the design questionnaire (Q6). Altitude gets two channels: a continuous gradient ramp for precision, and a shadow cast onto the chart for instinct. Each aircraft throws a shadow whose offset and blur scale with altitude — a plane on the deck has its shadow tucked underneath it, one at FL380 floats far off the page. Vertical rate rides the same channel for free: a closing shadow reads as a descent before any number does. Must be proven at real traffic density and in both themes.
design: Open question the prototype must answer: how does a cast shadow work on a DARK chart (see the night-edition bead)? Shadow may need to become a glow, a halo, or an offset ghost — but the parallax-lift semantics must survive whatever it becomes. The aircraft layer is NOT React (see CLAUDE.md) — this lives in the MapLibre layer, so the shadow is a second symbol/circle layer driven by the same GeoJSON source, offset per-feature.
acceptance_criteria: At real feeder density, in both themes, a glance distinguishes high from low traffic without reading a single number, and a climbing aircraft is visibly climbing.
close_reason: Cast shadow PROVED and shipped behind style/shadows-enabled? (commit c6f5221). Technique: icon-translate is constant-only in MapLibre 4.7, so the offset is a per-feature property (adsb.geo/shadow-offset — pure, unit-tested cljc) consumed by data-driven icon-offset; because icon-offset rides the icon's rotated frame, geo counter-rotates the fixed NW-sun SE throw by each track — one sun over the whole chart, verified for arbitrary tracks in geo_test. Curve: sqrt(alt/40000) x 30 icon-px — half throw by 10,000 ft so the low sky's drama stays legible; ground/absent altitude cast NOTHING (property omitted, layer filtered on has shadow-offset); shadow opacity multiplies the shared age fade so it dies with its plane. Blur: pre-blurred SDF silhouettes (crisp icons have binary alpha — halo-blur needs a gradient) + altitude-driven icon-halo-width/blur deepening the penumbra. HONEST VERDICT at replay density (39 positioned), headless Chromium, both editions: day is a clear WIN — FL300+ shadows float a glyph-length off the page, low traffic keeps them tucked, Tampa cluster stays legible, no smudge; night READS but quieter — the night paper leaves little room to darken, shadows legible at lean-in over land and water (consistent with Q15c lean-in stance) though weaker than day; recommend keeping and letting the visual pass tune night alphas ([0 .55][40k .32] now). Screenshots: scratchpad shadow-day.png / shadow-night.png + crops. bb test 632+814 assertions green, bb lint clean. Same GeoJSON source, no React, no extra setData — 60fps unthreatened.

title: Prototype the night edition of The Sectional
description: Q2c (follow the system theme) is the biggest risk in the chosen direction. The Sectional was drawn as warm paper (#F5EFDF) and nothing else; it now has to carry a dark theme too. A night edition must be DESIGNED, not derived by inverting or filtering the day one — hypsometric terrain tints, contour lines, ink silhouettes, and the aviation-magenta/aero-blue accents all have to be re-reasoned for a dark ground. Prove it in a running prototype before committing the palette.
design: Not a filter over the day theme. Think 'chart under a red-lit cockpit lamp' or 'chart printed on dark stock', not 'inverted PNG'. Both themes must satisfy Q4c (richly rendered basemap) and Q3c (commit to the bit).
acceptance_criteria: A running prototype shows the same map and chrome in both light and dark, and both look deliberate — neither reads as the other one's leftovers.
close_reason: Night edition PROVED in the running app (commit 43edbc3). Mechanism: adsb.map.theme (prefers-color-scheme seam, ratom, watch), adsb.map.basemap (pure palette-driven re-inking of the fetched Liberty JSON, keyed on source-layer+type; shields/pattern fills hidden at night, natural-earth raster hidden in both editions; OpenFreeMap's hosted dark/fiord examined and rejected — generic darks, not our artifact; sustainable path recorded in doc §3), adsb.map.style restructured into :day/:night palettes (single source of truth — legend follows via the theme ratom, halo = each edition's own paper, trail head opacity 0.5), adsb.map.view fetches the plate once and re-prints map+layer on flip, app.css custom-properties layer (rode in with the Stack commit 58ba374) themes the chrome. Verified against replay traffic in headless Chromium under BOTH emulated color schemes: night reads as a printed night chart, not an invert. One palette amendment proved: night roads at ~0.6 alpha (full-strength #6B5540 glowed like filament) — doc §2 table updated. bb test 555+742 assertions green, bb lint clean.

title: Load .env at the composition root (adsb.env), not the launcher
description: A JVM app cannot see .env; System/getenv reports the process environment only. Loading it in bb.edn only worked when launched via 'bb dev'. adsb.env/read! backfills the process environment from .env at the composition root, so every entry point (uberjar, bb dev, bare clojure -M:dev, IDE, REPL) boots identically. A real exported variable always beats the file, and production ships no .env, so it cannot shadow deployed config.
acceptance_criteria: A bare 'clojure -M:dev' picks up ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL from .env; an exported var overrides the file; missing .env is a no-op
close_reason: adsb.env/read! backfills the process environment from .env at main's composition root. env->config was already pure and took the env map as an argument, so it was fed rather than changed. bb.edn reverted. Verified from a bare 'clojure -M' with bb absent: System/getenv nil, env/read! yields the .env value, an exported var overrides it. bb test:clj 116/460 green, bb test:cljs 479 green, bb lint clean.

title: bb.edn: load .env into the dev process environment
description: A .env file is inert for a JVM app — System/getenv only sees the exported shell env, and nothing on the classpath parses .env. Load it in bb.edn (the project's command surface) and pass it to child processes via :extra-env so 'bb dev' works from a fresh clone without a shell incantation. Real environment variables must take precedence over the file so CI/Docker overrides still win.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev picks up ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL from .env; a real exported env var overrides the file; missing .env is a no-op, not an error
close_reason: bb.edn :init parses .env and passes it to the dev task's child processes via :extra-env. Real exported env wins over the file; missing .env is a clean no-op. Verified all three cases with a temporary probe task (since bb dev's ports were occupied). Caught two EDN-reader traps along the way: bb.edn allows neither #"regex" literals nor @deref.

title: shadow-cljs.edn: defer classpath to deps.edn via :deps
description: shadow-cljs.edn duplicates deps (malli 0.17.0 declared in both files, free to drift). Set :deps {:aliases [:cljs]} in shadow-cljs.edn and move CLJS deps/source-paths into a :cljs alias in deps.edn so there is one classpath source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: bb test:cljs passes; bb lint clean; malli declared in exactly one place
close_reason: shadow-cljs.edn now carries :deps {:aliases [:cljs]}; CLJS deps + test paths moved to a :cljs alias in deps.edn. malli declared once. bb test:cljs green (45 tests / 228 assertions), bb test:clj green (93/363), bb lint clean.

title: AUTEUR: write docs/design-questionnaire.md
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: rather than picking from the three proposed directions (Nocturne / The Sectional / Terminal) outright, AUTEUR writes docs/design-questionnaire.md - a set of questions about the design of the app whose answers will pick or at least narrow the direction. The questionnaire should discriminate between the live proposals and probe taste beyond them: mood, light/dark, information density, motion tolerance, typography leanings, how loud emergencies should be, phone vs desktop primacy, etc. Overseer answers at a later time; adsb-bvi.5 (decide the direction) then consumes the answers. This also restores the design-questionnaire.md artifact that archetypes/AUTEUR.md originally referenced.
acceptance_criteria: docs/design-questionnaire.md exists, committed; questions are answerable asynchronously (each with concrete options or examples, not open-ended essays); answers would demonstrably discriminate between the three live proposals; adsb-bvi.5 references the questionnaire.
close_reason: docs/design-questionnaire.md written and committed (d7fd06e): 15 async-answerable checkbox questions in 6 groups (mood/light, map's voice, density/chrome, motion, emergency loudness, typography/glanceability), each option tagged (N)/(S)/(T) where it echoes a candidate, closing with a how-answers-map-to-candidates note incl. likely hybrids. adsb-bvi.5 notes now reference it and wait on the Overseer's answers.

title: Go-live: provision host, DNS, first deploy, smoke check
description: The last mile that no other bead owns: provision the cloud host, point DNS at it, obtain the TLS cert, run the first real deploy, and smoke-check the full path - antenna to ultrafeeder to tunnel to backend to SSE to a phone on cellular data. The testing docs forbid live-feeder tests; this is not a test, it is the operational acceptance of the whole system.
acceptance_criteria: The app is reachable at its public HTTPS domain from off-network; live aircraft render and move; the connection indicator behaves through a deliberate tunnel bounce; a runbook section in the README covers redeploy and rollback.
notes: PIVOT (Overseer 2026-07-12): DigitalOcean App Platform, not a droplet. DNS: map.bwawan.com CNAME to the App Platform app; TLS by the platform; container built from the repo Dockerfile or buildpack per .do spec. Cloudflared tunnel + service token steps unchanged (feeder side). Prior droplet/compose/Caddy instructions superseded pending adsb-kh4.7-class retarget bead. Also pending: Overseer blessing of the unlicensed tar1090-db enrichment data (earlier note).
close_reason: Go-live is DONE — the app is deployed, public, and serving. Provisioned on DigitalOcean App Platform; map.bwawan.com resolves over TLS with the app's own security headers (verified 2026-07-14); the feeder rides an Access-gated Cloudflare Tunnel (adsb-sil); SSE survives both proxy layers at ~1Hz (adsb-ju1); the README now covers redeploy and rollback (commit 14266a7). One acceptance item was not an outcome of deploying but an experiment on the running system — the deliberate tunnel bounce — and it is carried forward as adsb-3el rather than held open here, since nothing else about go-live is pending on it.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Runbook retargeted (adsb-kh4.7): README 'Deployment' now leads with App Platform (doctl apps create --spec .do/app.yaml), compose+Caddy demoted to documented alternative. Two things go-live MUST measure rather than assume:

1. ADSB_TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS. The spec ships 1 (the compose/Caddy value). Deploy, hit the app from a known address, read the X-Forwarded-For the container received, count the entries to the RIGHT of your own address and add one. Too low => every visitor buckets under DO's internal address and the 5th stranger gets a 503; too high => the per-IP cap keys on client-chosen bytes and is spoofable. README 'The client address' documents the procedure.
2. SSE through DO's router — adsb-ju1 (P0) owns this. ADSB_SOURCE=replay proves the whole path with no feeder in the loop.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Runbook criterion SATISFIED 2026-07-14 — README 'Redeploy and rollback' now exists (it did not; the section titled 'Runbook' was the tunnel SETUP procedure, and the word 'rollback' appeared nowhere in README, docs/, or .do/).

What it records, per the Overseer's decision: rollback is git (revert or reset+push to master, which deploy_on_push turns into a deploy). The section documents that, plus the two things the git answer alone leaves out:

* A push to master is a FULL container build (shadow-cljs release + uberjar on DO's builders), so 'revert and push' means running broken for the length of a build. The dashboard's Activity -> Rollback re-runs an ALREADY-BUILT image and is as fast as a restart. Documented as two moves in order: stop the bleeding with the image rollback, then fix forward in git.
* git revert is preferred over reset --hard + push --force. Both deploy. The force-push additionally rewrites the history every deployment record points at, so the deploy log ends up naming commits that no longer exist and the incident becomes unreconstructable.

Also noted there: env/secret changes are a spec update (doctl apps update), not a code deploy; and rotating the feeder service token in Cloudflare while the old ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_* secrets are live empties the map while the app still reports healthy — /healthz carries feeder-status, so that is the thing to watch.

STILL OPEN on this bead, and only the Overseer can do it: the deliberate tunnel bounce. Drop cloudflared at home, watch the connection indicator degrade and recover from an off-network client. Everything else in the acceptance criteria is confirmed — public HTTPS reachability (2026-07-14), live aircraft rendering and SSE at ~1Hz through both proxy layers (adsb-ju1, closed).

title: Production basemap tile source
description: The dev map can lean on a free demo style, but MapLibre demo tiles are explicitly not for production, and this app will be internet-facing. Pick a sustainable, no-token tile source - OpenFreeMap, self-hosted PMTiles/Protomaps, or similar - verify its usage policy tolerates public traffic, and wire correct attribution. If any provider requiring a key is chosen instead, that key becomes the first browser-visible secret and security-checklist.md section 3 must be updated (the docs currently promise there are none).
design:
PROVIDER DECISION: OpenFreeMap 'liberty' style — https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty

RESEARCH / RATIONALE (evaluated 2026-07-11):
- OpenFreeMap (CHOSEN): public instance is free, no API key, no registration, no cookies. Terms (openfreemap.org) state NO limits on map views/requests, commercial use allowed, sustainable for a public hobby site (donation/sponsor funded). Zero browser-visible secret => security-checklist.md §3's 'no tokens' promise stays true, no doc change needed. Attribution 'OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap' is carried in the style JSON and rendered automatically by MapLibre's attribution control (already enabled). 'liberty' is the neutral-rich variant (terrain/water/labels) matching the richly-rendered design lean.
- Protomaps self-hosted PMTiles: strongest for zero external dependency, but needs us to serve tiles/PMTiles ourselves — deferred as the documented fallback if OpenFreeMap's public instance ever degrades. No domain impact (only the style URL changes).
- CARTO / MapLibre demotiles: demotiles are explicitly NOT for production; CARTO basemaps have usage/attribution constraints and lean toward keyed access. Rejected.

IMPLEMENTATION: option (a) — single style URL in dev and prod (fair use permits it), replacing the DEV-ONLY demotiles URL. Renamed adsb.map.view/dev-style-url -> style-url. attributionControl stays true. No :closure-defines, no runtime config, no index.html injection needed. VARIANT (not provider) may be swapped by adsb-dgb.5 by editing the one URL.

VERIFICATION: no token/key string in built main.js (grep clean); existing map tests assert style is https + token-free (still green); manual bb dev visual check of style load + attribution.
acceptance_criteria: Production style loads from the chosen source within its usage policy; attribution is correct; either no token ships to the browser, or the docs are updated to reflect the first one.
close_reason: Production basemap set to OpenFreeMap 'liberty' (https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty): no-token, no-key, unlimited public instance, commercial use allowed — fair use permits one URL in dev and prod, so no browser-visible secret ships and security-checklist §3 stays true (no doc change needed). Attribution 'OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap' carried by the source TileJSON, rendered automatically by MapLibre's attribution control. Verified: release main.js grep shows the OpenFreeMap URL baked in, demotiles gone, zero token/key strings; headless-Chromium render loaded the style + 15 vector tiles, attribution control reads 'MapLibre | OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap', Florida basemap rendered. bb test 130 tests/581 assertions green, bb lint 0/0. Commit 2a56d76. Protomaps self-hosted PMTiles documented as the fallback if the public instance degrades. Variant (not provider) may be swapped by adsb-dgb.5.

title: Replay Source: run the app and tests from the recorded fixture
description: A Source implementation that replays the recorded aircraft.json fixture on a loop with advancing timestamps. Two reasons it must exist: the docs forbid testing against a live feeder, so ingest and stream tests need a deterministic Source; and bb dev should work on a laptop that cannot reach the home network. This is also the proof that the Source protocol seam actually holds.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev renders a moving map with no feeder reachable, driven by the replayed fixture; ingest and SSE tests consume the replay Source; replayed seen ages advance so staleness logic is exercised.
close_reason: Added adsb.ingest.replay: a fixture-backed Source (open!/fetch!/close!) replaying test/resources/aircraft-sample.json forever through an injected monotonic clock — positions dead-reckon along track at ground speed, seen ages accrue at half the clock so aircraft cross the 60s stale line without reaching the 300s age-out, per-lap reset keeps the picture full. Wired via ADSB_SOURCE=replay (no feeder URL required; ultrafeeder remains default and still fails loud on a missing/bad URL). Unit tests use a fake clock (advancing seen, moving positions, looping, determinism); config/main tests cover source selection. bb test:clj (93 tests, 363 assertions) and bb lint clean. Verified end-to-end: ADSB_SOURCE=replay clojure -M:dev -> /healthz feeder-status ok, SSE snapshot 39 positioned aircraft, later update 38 moved, some stale, zero false position-suspect flags. RESIDUAL: the acceptance 'bb dev renders a moving map' cannot be shown visually yet because the map aircraft layer (adsb-2yu.4) does not exist; the SSE-level proof of moving/aging aircraft is the honest equivalent today. Skipped bb test:cljs to avoid colliding with the concurrent src/cljs agent; my change is clj-only. Commit 0b403f4. Not pushed.

title: CI: run bb test and bb lint on every push
description: GitHub Actions workflow: JVM + babashka + Node setup, Playwright browser install for the CLJS suite, bb test and bb lint as required checks. The CLJS suite runs in real headless Chromium in CI exactly as locally - do not let CI quietly downgrade to jsdom.
acceptance_criteria: The workflow runs bb test and bb lint on push and PR; the CLJS suite runs in real headless Chromium in CI; a deliberately failing cljs test fails the build.
notes: CI must install Playwright browsers for the CLJS suite
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml: temurin 21 + setup-clojure (Clojure CLI + babashka) + Node 20, m2/gitlibs/npm/playwright caching, npm ci, playwright install chromium --with-deps, then bb lint + bb test on push to master and PR. CLJS suite runs in real headless Chromium via script/run-browser-tests.mjs (no jsdom). Workflow compiles the :app build before bb test because the JVM static-serving route test needs the gitignored resources/public/js/main.js. Verified locally against HEAD in a throwaway git worktree (bb lint clean; bb test = 9 clj/cljc + 17 cljs browser assertions, all pass once app compiled). actionlint 1.7.12 reports the YAML clean. NOT observed running on GitHub: I cannot push, so remote CI green is unproven. Commit 3c85e3b.

title: Cloud-to-home tunnel for feeder connectivity
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the cloud backend reaches the home ultrafeeder over a VPN/tunnel (Tailscale, WireGuard, or cloudflared - pick one and document why). ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL points at the tunnel address; the feeder is never exposed publicly. Verify boot-time URL validation and the poll-loop backoff behave sanely when the tunnel is down.
design:
Architecture: Cloudflare Tunnel, service-token pattern (confirmed against current Cloudflare One docs, 2026-07).

DIRECTION: cloud backend reaches IN to the home feeder; the feeder is reachable from nowhere else. cloudflared runs on the HOME network (beside the ultrafeeder), dialing out to Cloudflare — no home inbound port, no public IP. It exposes dietpi:8100 as a private hostname (feeder.bwawan.com on the Overseer's bwawan.com zone) gated by a Cloudflare Access 'Service Auth' policy, so the hostname is NOT publicly readable without the token.

AUTH: the cloud app is a plain HTTPS client of feeder.bwawan.com. It presents a Cloudflare Access service token as CF-Access-Client-Id / CF-Access-Client-Secret headers on EVERY feeder request (aircraft.json AND receiver.json), sourced from ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_ID / ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_SECRET.

Alternatives rejected: (a) 'cloudflared access tcp' on the cloud side — adds a second daemon + localhost hop for no gain over an authenticated GET; (b) WARP/WireGuard — puts the whole cloud host on the home LAN, far more reach than 'read one JSON file' needs. Service token grants exactly the needed reach.

CODE: adsb.ingest.config/feeder-auth-headers builds the header map, validated both-or-neither at boot (half a credential fails loudly, like a bad URL); secret NEVER in exception message/ex-data. Threaded config -> ultrafeeder/->source -> fetch-batch! and receiver/fetch-position!.

FAILURE MODE: downed tunnel / revoked token / missing header => non-2xx from the edge => poll loop backs off + UI shows feeder unhealthy (already covered by poll backoff tests). Safe by construction.

RESIDUALS (go-live adsb-kh4.6, Overseer's Cloudflare account): create tunnel, ingress to dietpi:8100, Access app + Service Auth policy, generate token, curl verify with/without headers. No real tunnel was created here; runbook in README Deployment -> 'Cloud-to-home feeder tunnel'.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen tunnel is documented with rationale; the cloud container polls the home feeder over it; a downed tunnel behaves exactly like a feeder outage - backoff on the server, honest status in the UI; the feeder is reachable from nowhere else.
notes: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: cloudflared (Cloudflare Tunnel) from the home server. Pairs with DNS/TLS on Cloudflare if that is where the domain lands.
close_reason: Cloudflare Tunnel + Access service-token auth implemented and documented. config/feeder-auth-headers (both-or-neither boot validation, secret never logged) threaded config -> ultrafeeder + receiver fetches; compose sidecar stub replaced with home-side note + auth env placeholders; README runbook added. Tests: headers present/absent + secret-never-leaks-on-failure. bb test:clj (534) + bb test:cljs (618) + bb lint all clean. Commit a5faf7c. Operational tunnel/Access provisioning is go-live residual (adsb-kh4.6); no real tunnel created. main.clj env-threading edit left in working tree, riding with the Overseer's uncommitted dotenv work (not committed here).
