title: Production build: release CLJS + runnable jar
description: Make bb build real: shadow-cljs release build with optimizations, backend packaged as an uberjar (or clojure -X entry point) that serves the compiled frontend and reads all config from the environment. The artifact the Docker image will wrap.
acceptance_criteria: bb build produces an optimized CLJS release and a runnable jar that serves it; all config comes from the environment; the jar boots and serves the live map against a reachable feeder.
close_reason: bb build now produces a deployable artifact. Added src/clj/adsb/main.clj (production -main: reads PORT [default 8280] and ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL from env — URL captured, not yet used, for adsb-nqf.1 to validate; starts adsb.http.server; blocks), build.clj + :build alias (tools.build) that AOTs adsb.main and packages resources/ incl. the shadow release frontend into target/adsb.jar, and a bb build task that runs 'shadow-cljs release app' before cutting the jar. Verified end-to-end from a clean worktree at the commit (1afc7cf): bb build -> target/adsb.jar (~19MB, 0 warnings); java -jar on an ephemeral PORT -> /healthz 200 JSON, / serves index.html (302->/index.html->200), /js/main.js serves the optimized release bundle (1.14MB), unknown icao 404s, clean shutdown. bb test:clj (19 tests/108 assertions, incl. adsb.main-test) and bb lint (0/0) pass. RESIDUAL: the acceptance clause 'serves the live map against a reachable feeder' is NOT met — feeder ingest/aircraft-store wiring (adsb-nqf.1/nqf.2) is not yet connected into the running server, so the map has no live aircraft. Everything else in the acceptance criteria is met. Committed via pathspec in 1afc7cf; note deps.edn/bb.edn also carry a concurrent task's nrepl-alias edits, swept in by whole-file pathspec (preserved, not reverted).

title: Dockerfile + compose for cloud deployment
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile (deps cache, build, slim JRE runtime), a compose file for the cloud host, health check wired to the health endpoint, and config via environment. Document the deploy loop in the README.
acceptance_criteria: The multi-stage image builds with a slim JRE runtime; compose up serves the app; the container healthcheck hits the health endpoint; the deploy loop is documented in the README.
close_reason: Multi-stage Dockerfile (stage1: clojure:temurin-21-tools-deps-bookworm + Node20 + pinned babashka, cache-warmed from deps.edn/package.json/shadow-cljs.edn then bb build -> target/adsb.jar; stage2: eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine, non-root adsb user, jar-only), EXPOSE 8280, HEALTHCHECK via busybox wget on /healthz, env-only config. compose.yaml: app service (env incl. ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL tunnel placeholder, restart unless-stopped, port map) + documented commented cloudflared sidecar stub w/ TODO(adsb-kh4.3). .dockerignore + README Deployment section (build/run loop + env table). VERIFIED: bb build succeeds from clean (exit 0, 30MB jar); the jar run as the container would (java -jar, ADSB_SOURCE=replay) serves /healthz 200 {status ok,feeder-status ok}, / 302, and SSE snapshot+update frames. UNVERIFIED (honest residual): the Docker DAEMON is not running on this Mac (client 29.6.1 present, socket absent), so the image was NOT built/run and hadolint is not installed. Dockerfile validated by review only; first real docker build should confirm babashka install-script + NodeSource apt steps and busybox wget healthcheck on the target arch. Commit cbf7a74. Not pushed; concurrent-agent edits to deps.edn/main.clj/view.cljs etc. left unstaged.

title: Smooth interpolation between updates (dead reckoning)
description: Aircraft receive positions at ~1 Hz and must glide, not jump: between SSE ticks, project each positioned aircraft forward along track at ground speed via requestAnimationFrame, snapping to truth when a real update lands. Runs in the imperative map path - this must never touch React. Skip aircraft without gs/track; absent is not zero.
acceptance_criteria: Aircraft glide between ticks via rAF projection along track at ground speed and snap when a real update lands; aircraft without gs or track do not move; projection stops for stale aircraft; the map stays smooth.
close_reason: Aircraft glide between 1 Hz updates via a rAF dead-reckoning loop: pure spherical destination math + project-aircraft in adsb.geo (cljc, unit-tested against the canonical worked example), integrated in the imperative layer as a third push path off the shared !state cache — aircraft source only, trails stay real, ~20 Hz push throttle, pauses on hidden tab, stops on detach, snaps to truth on real frames, holds gs/track-less and stale aircraft. Zero React involvement proven by a rAF zero-re-render test. Full bb test (581 assertions) + lint clean. Commit aa27786.

title: Position trails with fading opacity
description: Per-aircraft position history kept client-side for the session (no persistence, per Overseer decision), rendered as a MapLibre line layer with opacity fading toward the tail. Cap history length per aircraft so memory is bounded; trails vanish when the aircraft ages out.
acceptance_criteria: A moving aircraft leaves a trail fading toward the tail; per-aircraft history is capped so memory is bounded; the trail is removed when the aircraft ages out; no React involvement.

title: Header, legend, connection status indicator

Wired into views/app-root. Browser tests green (counts render+update, three indicator states, legend swatches equal style constants); bb test + bb lint clean. Commit b6af0c7.

title: Aircraft list sidebar with sort and filters
description: A Reagent sidebar listing current aircraft with sort (altitude, distance, callsign) and simple filters (e.g. positioned-only, airborne-only). Click-to-select syncs with the map. Driven by re-frame subs over the same aircraft state the map reads - one source of truth.

close_reason: Aircraft list sidebar shipped (src/cljs/adsb/ui/sidebar.cljs + test). Reagent roster deriving from the same :aircraft/picture sub the map reads — one source of truth. Rows: callsign (icao fallback), three-state altitude (dash for absent, never 0), ground speed; badges for emergency squawk (adsb.geo/emergency?), position-suspect, MLAT. Delegated click dispatches existing [:aircraft/select icao]; selected row marked via :aircraft/selected-icao. ACCEPTANCE DEVIATION: distance sort dropped — it presumed a receiver reference, and the receiver position is PRIVATE by design and never reaches the browser; the only alternative (map-center distance) needs map-state plumbing outside this bead and isn't a true distance-from-antenna. Substituted seen-age sort. Full bb test (82 tests, 388 assertions) + bb lint clean. Commit ba33677 covers the two new files. Root mount in views.cljs and neutral CSS in app.css are applied on disk but left UNSTAGED: both entangle with a concurrent header/legend agent's uncommitted work; committing here would capture their WIP or reference not-yet-committed namespaces (adsb.ui.header/legend). Whoever owns views.cljs/app.css commits the mount.

title: Client-side staleness: fade and remove silent aircraft

notes: Scope addition from 2yu.5 residual: adsb.geo/aircraft->feature does not yet lift :aircraft/mlat? onto feature properties - add :mlat (boolean, omitted when absent) + test, so the style layer can express lower confidence. Style constants live in adsb.map.style; fade should be expression-driven off the stale/age property, constants in that ns.

Commit b2bfbbf.

title: Aircraft selection + detail panel

Selection survives SSE updates and clears when the aircraft ages out. Remember Boundary 4: every string in this panel came off an unauthenticated radio.

Panel renders callsign/icao/altitude/speed/track/squawk/vrate/seen-age with absent->em-dash (never 0), suspect+mlat badges, close button. Boundary-4 XSS-proof test passes: hostile callsign renders as escaped text, no img node. Selection survives updates, closes when aircraft leaves picture; dangling icao pruned by 1Hz UI-edge clock tick. 321 cljs + 370 clj assertions green, lint clean, app build clean. Commit bd77c2e.

title: Data-driven aircraft styling: heading rotation, altitude ramp, emergency state
description: MapLibre layer style expressions - not per-feature code: icon rotated by track, color ramped by altitude, distinct emergency treatment driven by feature properties. Altitude, heading, and emergency state are the data that carry meaning (AUTEUR); make them legible at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Icons rotate with track, color follows the altitude ramp, and emergency aircraft are visually distinct - all via style expressions over feature properties, no per-feature JS; the plane icon asset is registered in the style (sprite or addImage).

Icon: two SDF silhouettes (plane + dot for track-less) via seam add-image!, icon-rotate [get track], alignment map. Three-state altitude ramp (absent/ground/numeric) with has-guards; emergency overrides colour+size; stale fades opacity. Click contract dispatches [:aircraft/select icao] through the seam (event/UI owned by dgb.1). Tests + lint green (commit 39b7d1b).

title: Plausibility layer: range gate + position-jump flagging
description: The second validation layer from validation-boundaries.md, beyond per-field limits: drop positions beyond the receiver horizon (needs receiver position - read it from ultrafeeder receiver.json or env), drop absurd fields while keeping the aircraft, and FLAG impossible position jumps rather than dropping them - a jump is the fingerprint of spoofing and must surface, not vanish. Requires prior state, so it sits on the state store.
acceptance_criteria: A position beyond the receiver horizon drops the aircraft; an absurd altitude or speed drops the field and keeps the aircraft; an impossible position jump is flagged, not dropped, and surfaces in the data; values are never silently clamped.
notes: Live probe shows the feeder emits r_dst / r_dir (receiver-relative distance and bearing) per aircraft - useful for the range gate server-side. PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): receiver position is hidden from the public site, and r_dst/r_dir must NEVER reach the wire format - one aircraft's position plus its r_dst/r_dir locates the antenna exactly. Strip them at ingest or at serialization, and test for their absence.
close_reason: Plausibility layer built and committed (3cd83da). Range gate: adsb.ingest.plausibility/gate-range drops aircraft strictly beyond a configurable horizon (default 400 km / ~216 nm); position-less aircraft pass; nil receiver position disables the gate. Receiver position: adsb.ingest.receiver/resolve-position! once at setup - ADSB_RECEIVER_LAT/LON override wins, else feeder receiver.json (shape verified against the live feeder), else nil + one warn log; coordinates never logged. Jump flagging: >1200 kt sustained implied speed sets :aircraft/position-suspect? true - flagged never dropped/clamped; recomputed per observation (consistent follow-up clears; position-less observation inherits flag with inherited position); composed into adsb.state/apply-batch!. Schema: :aircraft/position-suspect? optional boolean. Privacy tested: coerce carries no trace of r_dst/r_dir; pipeline yields only aircraft-namespaced keys; committed receiver fixture uses synthetic coords. Docs updated (validation-boundaries.md plausibility section + receiver-position sourcing + privacy). bb test (clj 70/287 + cljs browser 37/189) and bb lint clean. Boot wiring for gate-range left as a bead comment for the SSE/integration bead (main.clj owned elsewhere).

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Boot wiring left for the SSE/integration bead (main.clj/deps owned elsewhere this session). Exact wiring when the poller is started:

  ;; once, at poller setup - never per poll:
  (def receiver-position
    (receiver/resolve-position! {:env (System/getenv) :base-url feeder-url}))
  ;; nil => range gate disabled, already logged once by resolve-position!

  ;; in the poll callback:
  :on-batch! (fn [batch]
               (-> batch
                   (plausibility/gate-range receiver-position
                                            plausibility/default-max-range-m)
                   (state/apply-batch! (System/currentTimeMillis))))

Namespaces: adsb.ingest.receiver (clj edge), adsb.ingest.plausibility (pure cljc). Jump flagging needs no wiring - it is already composed into adsb.state/apply-batch!. PRIVACY: never log or serialize receiver-position; env override ADSB_RECEIVER_LAT/LON wins over receiver.json.

Residual: adsb.fixtures mlat-derived comment says nqf.3 would surface MLAT confidence as a domain field - that was not in this bead's scope; needs its own bead if wanted.

Covers production build, image, tunnel connectivity, and the hardening that internet exposure demands beyond what the docs currently assume.

Build them against the real Malli schemas so a schema change forces them to update.

title: Fill empty stack sections in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md
description: CLAUDE.md has empty placeholder sections: 'Build & Test', 'Architecture Overview', 'Conventions & Patterns'. Fill them with the decided stack and mirror into AGENTS.md per the repo's own note that the two files are independent and must be kept in sync.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stream status never escalates to :down when the backend is unreachable
description:
OWNER DECISION (2026-07-14): retries never stop — the browser keeps trying forever — but the STATUS shown to the reader escalates to :down after a few failed attempts, exactly as the namespace docstring already promises (past a few failed attempts we surface :down while still retrying).

The bug: when the backend is simply down (connection refused, process dead), EventSource fires error with readyState CONNECTING and owns the retry itself — it never reaches CLOSED. The :stream/error :connecting branch (src/cljs/adsb/stream.cljs:335-344) only sets :stream/connection :reconnecting and never increments :stream/attempts, so status-for-attempts / down-after-attempts never engage: the header shows :reconnecting forever in the most common outage. The :closed path (HTTP-level fatal errors) is currently the only route to :down — so the same dead backend shows different labels depending on how it died.

Fix direction: count :connecting errors toward :stream/attempts for STATUS purposes only (the browser fires one error per failed retry, roughly every few seconds) — do NOT schedule a competing reconnect on that branch (the browser owns that retry; double-connecting is the failure mode the current split exists to prevent). Reset attempts on :stream/opened as the :closed path already does. Result: both failure modes escalate to :down on the same attempts threshold, retries continue forever on both, and recovery still snaps back to :live on the next successful open.

Verified context: the reconnect machine itself is sound (open! closes the prior EventSource, schedule-reconnect!/clear-timer! prevent double-connect) — this change must not disturb it. Test: N synthetic :connecting errors escalate status to :down without any :stream/connect! effect being fired; a subsequent :stream/opened resets to :live and zero attempts.
acceptance_criteria: Backend-down (CONNECTING-loop) escalates to :down after the same attempts threshold as the :closed path; no competing reconnect is scheduled from the :connecting branch; :stream/opened resets attempts and status; bb test:cljs green
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14, finding 12; direction chosen by owner: retry forever, surface outage status after a few failures.
close_reason: Every EventSource error now counts toward :stream/attempts, so the CONNECTING loop (backend absent) escalates to :down on the same threshold as CLOSED. No reconnect scheduled from the CONNECTING branch — the browser still owns that retry. New test a-backend-that-is-simply-down-says-so asserts escalation, zero scheduled backoffs, zero competing opens, and reset on :stream/opened. bb test:cljs green (215/1582), bb lint clean. Commit f2bbbde.

title: Theme flip discards the reader's camera and re-frames the crop boundary
description:
UX polish. The map view restyles on an OS light/dark change by destroying and recreating the whole MapLibre map (view.cljs reprint! -> unmount-map! -> mount-map!, src/cljs/adsb/map/view.cljs:188-252). Two losses in that teardown:

1. The new map is created with default-map-opts (boot center, zoom 7) — nobody captures the dying map's center/zoom/bearing.
2. crop/attach! gets fresh per-attach state with its framed-once latch unset (src/cljs/adsb/map/crop.cljs:101-106,121), so fit-bounds! fires again on the new map — the exact mid-session yank the latch's own docstring forswears.

Scenario: reader has panned to a specific aircraft at zoom 12; dusk slides the OS to dark; the chart teleports back to the crop-boundary opening framing, losing their place. (Selection and follow survive in app-db, which masks it only while following.)

Fix direction: add a camera getter to the maplibre seam (center/zoom/bearing); in reprint! read the dying map's camera and pass it into mount-map!'s opts; seed the new crop handle as already-framed (or hoist the framed-once latch above per-attach state). Test: fake-map reprint preserves the recorded camera and issues no second fit-bounds.
acceptance_criteria: After a theme flip the camera is exactly where the reader left it and no re-framing fit-bounds fires; bb test:cljs green
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14, verified by direct read. Owner: not high priority, but polish worth having.
close_reason: Camera carried across the theme re-print (maplibre/camera -> reprint! -> mount-map! opts); crop handle seeded :framed? so no second fit-bounds. Tests: view-test a-theme-flip-keeps-the-readers-place, crop-test seeded-latch. bb test:cljs + test:clj + lint green. Commit 610f51b.

title: Chrome micro-perf batch: emergencies sub, hover re-render, matchMedia per render, on-load! trap
description:
Four small verified frontend items, grouped as one sweep. None threatens the frame budget alone; together they are the remaining per-message chrome tax after the roster investigation (adsb-7pz).

(a) subs.cljs:124-130 — :aircraft/emergencies re-runs (filter + sort-by) over the whole picture on every upsert, even when the sky is calm and the result is [] every time (propagation dedupes by =, the compute does not). Guard with a cheap any-emergency scan or layer the sub.

(b) roster.cljs:591,676 — top-level @hovered deref re-renders the whole roster tree per mouse enter/leave transition (row-level = prop diffing bounds DOM work, but the full hiccup rebuild + N argv compares still run). Push hover down to a per-row sub if/when the roster feels heavy — coordinate with adsb-7pz.

(c) roster.cljs:309-317 — phone-stance? calls window.matchMedia twice per roster render (via handle-label and handle-aria-label) and is non-reactive (stale label until next unrelated render after a 640px crossing — self-heals, cosmetic). Cache the MediaQueryList at module level and read .matches.

(d) map/maplibre.cljs:116-117 — on-load! uses Map.loaded(), which is false during ANY tile load, and the load event fires once per map: a future client attaching after initial load would silently never wire up (no source, no layer, no error). Not currently reachable — every attach! runs before load — but a trap. Use isStyleLoaded()/styledata, or document the pre-load-only contract on the protocol method.

Also for completeness: selection.cljs:174-181 derefs :aircraft/picture unconditionally in its track (a per-message no-op wakeup when nothing is hovered) — cheap, fix only if touching the file anyway; and ui/follow.cljs:33 subscribes to the full :aircraft/selected for a button that needs only has-position + mode (~1 Hz trivial re-render) — leave unless convenient, and note the owner is actively reworking follow.
acceptance_criteria: Emergencies sub does no sort when no emergency exists; hover no longer re-renders the full roster (or explicitly deferred to adsb-7pz); matchMedia cached; on-load! semantics fixed or documented; bb test green
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14. Coordinate (b) with adsb-7pz and any follow rework the owner has in flight.

title: REPL/shutdown ergonomics: start! silently ignores options; in-flight dial survives close!; spurious shutdown warn
description:
Three small operator/REPL traps, none production-visible, grouped as one cleanup:

(a) src/clj/adsb/http/server.clj:44-57 — start! returns the running server via (or @server ...) without comparing options: a second main/start! in a REPL (without stop!) wires NEW poller/broadcaster instances while HTTP keeps serving handlers closed over the OLD broadcaster — two pollers race on adsb.state and /api/stream connects to a broadcaster nobody feeds. Fix: warn (or throw) when start! is called with differing options while a server runs.

(b) src/clj/adsb/ingest/tcp.clj:203-211 with 142-160 — close! during an in-flight dial closes the OLD (nil) connection and interrupts the reader, but Socket.connect is not interrupt-responsive: the dial completes and pumps until the next message or the 60s idle timeout. Fix: check running? between transport returning and consume!, closing the fresh connection if stopped.

(c) adsb.ingest.poll/poll-once! catches the InterruptedException from a stop-during-fetch as a feeder failure, logging a spurious Feeder-unreachable warn at every shutdown. Fix: let interrupt propagate or detect stopped state before logging.
acceptance_criteria: Double start! with different options warns; close! during dial releases the socket promptly; clean shutdown logs no feeder warning; bb test green

title: Broadcaster limits read System/getenv directly, ignoring .env-merged config
description:
adsb.env/read! exists so every entry point boots identically from .env-backfilled config, and main.clj resolves dev-csp?, origin-token, crop, receiver override, and feeder auth from that map — but broadcast/start! (src/clj/adsb/stream/broadcast.clj:698-708 env-limit/env-flag?) falls back to raw System/getenv for ADSB_SSE_MAX_CLIENTS, ADSB_SSE_MAX_PER_IP, ADSB_TRUST_FORWARDED_FOR, and ADSB_TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS, and main.clj:290-314 does not pass them as options. Values set only in .env are silently ignored for exactly these four knobs. No production impact (real env vars there); a dev/test trap that contradicts env.clj's premise.

Fix direction: resolve the four knobs in main.clj from the env/read! map and pass them as explicit options to broadcast/start!; keep the getenv fallback in broadcast for entry points that do not use env.clj, or remove it and make the option required at the composition root.
acceptance_criteria: Setting the four SSE knobs only in .env changes the running broadcaster's resolved limits; bb test green

title: Streaming Sources report no message counts: message-rate permanently absent in SBS/Beast mode
description:
Only UltrafeederSource implements adsb.ingest.source/Metadata. The SBS and Beast readers (src/clj/adsb/ingest/sbs.clj:244-251, src/clj/adsb/ingest/beast_source.clj:113-120) count nothing, so (:messages (source/metadata source)) is always nil on streaming deployments (consumed at main.clj:305-313) and the UI's message rate is permanently unknown — despite these Sources seeing every individual message (they are BETTER placed to count than the poll source). main.clj's own docstring claims stats come from the source's message-count side-channel, so the current state also contradicts the documentation.

Fix direction: an AtomicLong (or atom) incremented per decoded message in each reader, exposed via the existing Metadata protocol; also feeds the :feeder/silent-frames radio-death detection on streaming deployments, which currently has no signal there.
acceptance_criteria: On a replayed SBS and Beast feed, stats frames carry a real message rate; bb test green

title: Bound feeder HTTP response sizes — an untrusted feeder can OOM the JVM
description:
The feeder is documented untrusted, and the server caps inbound request line/body sizes — but its OWN fetches of aircraft.json (src/clj/adsb/ingest/ultrafeeder.clj:35-41) and receiver.json (src/clj/adsb/ingest/receiver.clj:80-89) slurp the full response into memory with only a time limit. A compromised feeder or an on-path attacker (LAN deployments dial plain http://) can stream an arbitrarily large body and OOM the process.

Fix direction: http-kit's client supports :filter (org.httpkit.client/max-body-filter n) — pick a generous bound (a busy aircraft.json is tens of KB; receiver.json is tiny; something like 8-16 MB leaves 100x headroom) and treat an over-limit response as a feeder failure on the existing backoff path, logged once per transition like other feeder faults.
acceptance_criteria: Oversized responses are rejected at the bound and surface as feeder failures without OOM; test with a fake oversized payload (never the live feeder); bb test green

title: wss dial lifecycle: new HttpClient per reconnect, timed-out connect futures never cancelled
description:
src/clj/adsb/ingest/wss.clj:147-158,168-175 (build-websocket!). Every reconnect builds a fresh java.net.http.HttpClient — its own selector thread and connection pool. When the connect future times out at .get, the CompletableFuture is not cancelled and a WebSocket that completes AFTER the timeout is never referenced or aborted: a live TCP connection plus its client's selector thread stay rooted. Against a slow/flapping edge redialing every reconnect-ms, these accumulate over hours.

Fix direction: create ONE HttpClient per Source (or per transport closure) and reuse it across dials; on TimeoutException, (.cancel future true) and add a whenComplete fallback that aborts a websocket established after the timeout.
acceptance_criteria: One HttpClient per Source lifetime; a timed-out dial leaves no live websocket or selector thread; bb test green
notes: PENNY audit 2026-07-14. Same file as adsb-kpd (zero-byte frame) — coordinate if worked together.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Fixed with one HttpClient per transport closure (= per Source, since main.clj builds the transport once) and a one-shot referee atom over the dial.

Finding worth keeping: (.cancel future true) — the fix direction in the description — would NOT have worked. Cancelling completes the CompletableFuture exceptionally, so a WebSocket the JDK establishes afterwards is dropped on the floor rather than handed back: the reference is lost and the leak is exactly what you were trying to prevent. Instead claim-dial!/abandon-dial! arbitrate: whichever end of the dial arrives second at the atom owns the abort.

Second finding: the JDK's WebSocket.Builder.connectTimeout already tears down a handshake that overruns it, so a slow server cannot be made to hit the leak on demand — an e2e test against one passes with or without the sweep (verified: it did). The remaining race is the future landing in the same instant .get gives up. That is tested at the referee level, both orderings, deterministically. Selector-thread count guards the client reuse (4 dials = 4 threads before, 1 after).

title: Record real SBS and Beast captures from the home feeder for fixture parity
description: The SBS (adsb-0in) and Beast (adsb-doh) Sources are being built against synthetic fixtures constructed from the documented wire formats, because dietpi.local did not resolve from the dev Mac on 2026-07-13. When next on the home network, record ~30s each of tcp/30003 (SBS text) and tcp/30005 (Beast binary) — e.g. 'nc -w 30 dietpi.local 30003 > sbs-capture.txt' — and add them under test/resources/ following the aircraft-sample.json precedent (real bytes, warts included, README entry documenting capture date and any quirks observed). SBS/Beast carry aircraft positions but no receiver-position fields, so no redaction pass is expected; verify that claim against the actual capture before committing. Then point the replay tests at the real captures (or add them alongside the synthetic ones).
acceptance_criteria: Real captures of 30003 and 30005 live in test/resources/ with README provenance notes; replay tests exercise them; no receiver-position data committed; bb test passes.
close_reason: Real captures committed (a801e5c) with replay test pinning boundary behavior; reviewed for receiver-position data (none exists in either format)

title: Beast Source: socket + framing + decode + shared accumulator
description:
The integration layer: an adsb.ingest.source/Source over TCP dietpi.local:30005 that composes the pieces — socket reader feeding bytes through Beast framing (adsb-doh.1), payloads through DF17 decode (adsb-doh.2), and the resulting :aircraft/* deltas through the shared per-ICAO accumulator (adsb-0qa) into state this Source owns. open!/fetch!/close! semantics, reconnect behavior, and the fixture-replay test pattern mirror the SBS Source (adsb-0in) — reuse its TCP skeleton rather than writing a second one.

fetch! returns the current coerced domain batch; nothing Beast-shaped leaks past this namespace. Never test against the live feeder — record a Beast byte capture and replay it.
acceptance_criteria: A Beast Source implements open!/fetch!/close!; a recorded Beast capture replayed through the full stack yields correct domain aircraft (position, velocity, callsign); disconnect/reconnect behaves like the SBS Source; swapping Sources requires no change outside ingest wiring; bb test and bb lint pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mode-S / DF17 decode: CRC-24, extended squitter, CPR position
description:
The hard middle of the Beast Source: decode raw Mode-S payloads (from adsb-doh.1 frames) into partial :aircraft/* deltas. Scope: CRC-24 validation first — a frame that fails parity is radio noise or an injection attempt and is dropped at this boundary (the feeder is untrusted; docs/validation-boundaries.md). Then DF17 extended squitter: identification (callsign), airborne velocity, and airborne position via CPR — the odd/even frame-pair global decode, plus the locally-unambiguous decode once a reference position exists.

GATE, decide first: own the decoder or use a library. CPR and CRC-24 are well-specified (ICAO Annex 10 / the ADS-B DO-260 lineage; dump1090's implementation is the de facto reference) but easy to get subtly wrong. If a maintained JVM library covers DF17 cleanly, wrap it behind this namespace; if not, implement with known-answer test vectors taken from published examples. Either way the choice must be invisible outside this namespace.

Pure: (payload-bytes, now-ms, cpr-state) -> {:delta ... :cpr-state ...}. No sockets, no atoms — the Source (adsb-doh.3) owns all state.
acceptance_criteria: CRC-24 rejects corrupted frames with tests proving it; DF17 callsign, velocity, and position decode against published known-answer vectors; CPR global decode from an odd/even pair and local decode from a reference both verified; out-of-range/garbage payloads yield nil, never throw; decode is pure with time and cpr-state as arguments; bb test and bb lint pass.
notes:
GATE DECISION: own the decoder. Survey: Clojars has zero ADS-B/Mode-S artifacts; Maven Central has exactly one, org.opensky-network:libadsb 3.4.0 (the java-adsb lineage) — last release 2021-03-16 (~5 years unmaintained) and GPL-3.0 licensed, which is a heavier obligation than this codebase carries anywhere else. Decisive technical mismatch: libadsb's PositionDecoder is a stateful Java object (per-ICAO state + wall-clock assumptions inside the library), so wrapping it cannot satisfy the bead's pure (payload-bytes, now-ms, cpr-state) -> {:delta :cpr-state} contract without fighting the library; CRC-24 + DF17 field extraction + CPR is a few hundred lines of well-specified bit work with published known-answer vectors (The 1090MHz Riddle, mode-s.org; dump1090 as reference impl). Implementing in src/cljc (precedent: adsb.geo, adsb.ingest.coerce do portable math/coercion in cljc).
IMPLEMENTED (own decoder, per gate decision above). Namespaces: adsb.ingest.cpr (src/cljc/adsb/ingest/cpr.cljc — pure CPR math: nl, global-position, local-position) and adsb.ingest.mode-s (src/cljc/adsb/ingest/mode_s.cljc — crc, airborne-velocity, decode). Public API for adsb-doh.3: (mode-s/decode payload now-ms cpr-state) -> {:delta <partial :aircraft/* map or nil> :cpr-state <map to thread into the next call>}; cpr-state is icao -> {:cpr/even <half> :cpr/odd <half> :cpr/reference {:cpr/position {:geo/lat :geo/lon} :cpr/heard-at-ms}}; every non-nil delta carries a valid :aircraft/icao. Consumed: DF17 and DF18 CF0-2 (CF1 icao gets the schema's ~ prefix); TC1-4 callsign, TC19 velocity (subtype 1/2 -> ground-speed-kt+track-deg gated by schema plausibility, baro-sourced VR -> baro-rate-fpm; GNSS VR dropped like coerce drops geom_rate), TC9-18/20-22 position (Q-bit baro alt; TC20-22 metres->ft; CPR global pair within 10s, local vs own reference aged out past aircraft/age-out-threshold-ms). Ignored, documented in ns docstring: mode-ac/short frames, DF4/5/11/20/21 (overlaid parity), TC5-8 surface, Q=0 Gillham, DF18 CF3+, subtype 3/4 airspeed/heading in the delta (decoded in airborne-velocity; schema vocabulary follow-up filed as adsb-qxl). Known-answer vectors (mode-s.org worked examples): 8D4840D6202CC371C32CE0576098 -> KLM1023; even 8D40621D58C382D690C8AC2863A7 + odd 8D40621D58C386435CC412692AD6 -> 52.2572021484375/3.91937255859375 + 38000ft (global AND local vs ref 52.258/3.918); velocity ST1 8D485020994409940838175B284F -> 159.20kt/182.88deg/-832fpm GNSS; ST3 8DA05F219B06B6AF189400CBC33F -> TAS 375kt/243.98deg/-2304fpm baro. CRC proven against all 112 single-bit flips; NL zone-crossing pairs (10.470/10.471 and 86.53 straddles) rejected; garbage of every shape -> nil delta, state untouched. bb test:clj 195 tests/1214 assertions green, bb lint 0 warnings.
close_reason: Closed

title: Beast framing: de-escape the 0x1a stream into typed frames
description:
The pure first layer of the Beast Source: turn the raw byte stream from port 30005 into typed frames. Beast framing is escape-based — 0x1a starts a frame, a literal 0x1a in the payload is doubled — with a one-byte type ('1' Mode-A/C, '2' short Mode-S, '3' long Mode-S), a 6-byte MLAT timestamp, and a signal-level byte before the payload.

Pure function over bytes: (bytes, carry) -> {:frames [...] :carry rest} so a socket reader can feed arbitrary chunk boundaries. No decoding of the Mode-S payload here — that is the next layer (DF17 decode). Fixture-testable with a recorded byte capture; hostile input (truncated frames, bad types, escape at end of chunk) must be dropped or carried, never thrown past the boundary.
acceptance_criteria: De-escaping handles doubled 0x1a, frames split across chunk boundaries, and truncated tails via carry; frame type, MLAT timestamp, signal level, and raw payload are extracted; malformed input is dropped without throwing; tests run from a recorded byte fixture; bb test and bb lint pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: Beast Source: binary Mode-S from ultrafeeder's port 30005
description:
Epic: a Source over ultrafeeder's Beast feed (TCP dietpi.local:30005) — the binary Mode-S framing carrying raw ADS-B payloads plus MLAT timestamps and signal level. Lowest latency and highest fidelity the container offers, at the cost of decoding raw frames.

Split into three children, each independently testable: adsb-doh.1 (Beast framing: 0x1a de-escape into typed frames, pure), adsb-doh.2 (Mode-S/DF17 decode: CRC-24, callsign/velocity, CPR position — gated on a library-vs-own decision recorded in that bead), adsb-doh.3 (integration: socket + framing + decode + the shared per-ICAO accumulator adsb-0qa, reusing the SBS Source's TCP skeleton from adsb-0in).

Standing rules: the Source is the trust boundary for its wire format; nothing Beast-shaped leaks past the seam; never test against the live feeder — replay a recorded capture.
acceptance_criteria: A Beast Source implements open!/fetch!/close!; frames are de-escaped and Mode-S decoded (at minimum DF17 position via CPR pair, velocity, callsign); hostile/truncated frames are dropped at the boundary with tests; a recorded Beast fixture drives the tests; bb test and bb lint pass.
close_reason: All three children closed (framing b986cb8, decode 860fd3d, integration 1d25597); epic acceptance met — full suite 222+ tests green at host gate

title: Over-cap SSE rejection surfaces as Cloudflare 504, not the app's 503 + Retry-After
description:
Found 2026-07-13 verifying the per-IP cap live. With the cap binding correctly (4 admitted, 5th refused), the 5th connection through map.bwawan.com returns HTTP 504 from Cloudflare (server: cloudflare, cf-ray present) in ~0.17s — far too fast to be a real gateway timeout. Directly against the app (broadcast_test/per-ip-connection-cap and total-connection-cap) the same rejection is a clean 503 with a Retry-After body, so the app is doing the right thing; the proxy chain is mistranslating it.

MECHANISM (likely): admission is decided in connect! INSIDE http-kit/as-channel's :on-open, i.e. AFTER the request has been upgraded to an SSE async channel. reject! then sends {:status 503} and closes that channel. To a direct client http-kit writes a valid 503 response; but Cloudflare (two layers, SSE-aware) sees a just-upgraded stream get an abrupt non-2xx + close and reports 504 upstream-failure to the client. So the over-limit visitor gets an ambiguous gateway error and NO Retry-After hint.

IMPACT: low — only a client opening >max-per-ip concurrent connections hits it, and it IS refused (no slot leak: try-register! declines before registering, so the count is unaffected). But it violates the tested 503+Retry-After contract and 504s are alarming/ambiguous in monitoring.

LIKELY FIX: decide admission BEFORE as-channel and return a plain Ring 503 response for the over-cap case, so Cloudflare sees an ordinary non-2xx to the GET rather than a post-upgrade send. Requires reserving the per-ip/total slot by client-ip synchronously in the handler (the atomic claim can stay in on-open as the authoritative gate, with the sync pre-check as the clean-reject fast path). Verify against the live deployment that the 5th now gets 503+Retry-After through Cloudflare, not 504.
acceptance_criteria: Through map.bwawan.com, the (max-per-ip + 1)th concurrent connection returns 503 with a Retry-After header (not 504), verified with curl; admitted clients keep streaming; no slot leak on rejection.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: Fix WRITTEN 2026-07-13, awaiting deploy + live verification. connect! now decides admission SYNCHRONOUSLY before http-kit/as-channel: an over-limit connect returns a plain Ring 503 (shared rejection-response, Retry-After intact) rather than upgrading to an SSE channel and sending the 503 over it. The atomic try-register! in :on-open stays as the authoritative gate for the rare race where the last slot goes between the sync read and the claim — that tail still 504s, but the every-over-cap-connect norm no longer does. Unit test proves connect! returns a {:status 503} map (not an upgrade) and claims no slot. Verify live: hold max-per-ip streams, then the next connect through map.bwawan.com must be 503 + Retry-After, NOT 504.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
DEFINITIVE DIAGNOSIS 2026-07-13 — the sync-reject fix (83d364a) is LIVE and does NOT resolve this. It is NOT app-fixable.

Proof the fix is deployed: the live /js/main.js contains the adsb-sod UI markers ('No messages', 'PLOTTED') and none of the old labels (RNG/MSG/AC), so build 16ad7c8 is running, and 83d364a is its ancestor.

With that fix live, the app returns a plain synchronous Ring 503 for an over-cap connect — and the app CONFIRMS it rejects: 'SSE connect rejected :ip-full for 2603:9000:...' (a real IPv6 client, keyed via CF-Connecting-IP — also reconfirms adsb-nnk works). Yet the client still receives a Cloudflare-synthesized '504 Gateway Timeout' (content-type text/plain, ~0.2s), identical over HTTP/1.1 AND HTTP/2. So the double-edge stack (our Cloudflare zone -> DO's own Cloudflare -> container) converts the app's fast 503-on-/api/stream into a 504. Almost certainly the edge treats /api/stream as a streaming endpoint (it serves text/event-stream on success) and reads a quick non-stream 503+close as an upstream failure.

CONCLUSION: the connection IS refused (cap works; protection intact). Only the error SHAPE is wrong (504 vs 503+Retry-After), and it originates in the edge, not the app. My async->sync change did not and cannot fix it. Re-scoping to P3.

OPTIONS:
  a) ACCEPT (recommended). P3 cosmetic. The cap protects; a rare over-cap client gets an ugly-but-refusing 504.
  b) Cloudflare Rate Limiting Rule on /api/stream -> clean 429 at the edge before the origin, and offloads the cap. Correct architecture, but Cloudflare dashboard config + must be tuned not to break real SSE, and the app cap stays as defense-in-depth.
  c) My sync-reject change (83d364a) is live, tested, harmless, and marginally cleaner (no channel upgrade for a doomed connect) but did NOT achieve its goal. Keep or revert — churn either way; leaning keep.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: DECISION 2026-07-13 (Overseer): ACCEPTED as a known limitation (option a). Parked at P3, not actively worked. The over-cap connection is refused (cap works, protection intact); the 504-vs-503 shape is edge behavior, not app-fixable, and only a rare over-cap abuser ever sees it. The sync-reject change (83d364a) stays — live, tested, harmless, marginally cleaner. Reopen/raise only if the Cloudflare Rate Limiting path (option b) is ever wanted for clean 429s at the edge.

title: Surface feeder status in the browser
description: The stream connection chip (dgb.3) tracks browser-to-server SSE health, but the feeder can be down while the stream is live - the UI then looks healthy while aircraft silently age out (the fade helps, but nothing says why). The backend already tracks poll/status (:ok/:down/:starting + last-success). Carry it to the client - simplest: a feeder-status field on the SSE envelope (wire.cljc + broadcast), a sub, and a second state on or beside the connection chip. Keep the two indicators semantically distinct: stream health vs feeder health.
acceptance_criteria: Killing the feeder (or tunnel) while the stream stays up visibly flips the UI to a feeder-down state within a few seconds; stream reconnect and feeder-down render distinguishably; wire envelope documented in wire.cljc docstring.
close_reason: Feeder health now rides every SSE frame: wire.cljc feeder->wire/wire->feeder allowlist (status+last-success only, error excluded), broadcast threads a :feeder thunk (main composes poll/status), client decodes to :feeder/status with a derived :feeder/health sub applying the unknowable rule (stream not :live -> :unknown), header shows a second feeder chip and relabels the stream chip's :down to 'Disconnected'. Full cljs suite (136 tests) + wire/broadcast clj tests + lint green; live-proof: real feeder -> feeder ok (36 aircraft), unreachable feeder -> feeder down while SSE stayed live, no error string on the wire. Committed 013610a.

title: Surface MLAT-derived confidence on domain aircraft
description: The mlat-derived cast member (adsb-bvi.4) exists, but coercion does not yet surface positioning confidence: a raw entry with type mlat / non-empty mlat array coerces to an ordinary positioned aircraft. Add e.g. :aircraft/mlat? (or a confidence field) at the boundary so the UI can render lower-confidence targets distinctly, update the fixture cast comment that mis-attributed this to adsb-nqf.3, and align the fixture's domain form.
acceptance_criteria: mlat-derived raw form yields a domain aircraft carrying the confidence marker; schema updated; fixture cast test asserts it; the stale comment in fixtures.cljc corrected.
close_reason: Coercion surfaces :aircraft/mlat? true for MLAT-derived positions (type=mlat OR non-empty mlat array); true-or-absent marker consistent with on-ground?/position-suspect?; schema, wire allowlist (both directions), docstrings, fixtures comment, and tests updated. bb test:clj + bb lint clean; browser suite verified via force-spawn compile + run-browser-tests.mjs (52 tests, 265 assertions, 0 failures). Commit 7965e32.

title: Receiver stats readout (no public receiver location)
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the receiver position is private and must not be derivable from the public site - no range rings, no receiver marker, no antenna-centered default view. This bead is therefore a stats readout only: current aircraft count, positioned count, max observed range as a number, and message rate if the feeder exposes it. The backend still knows the true position for the range gate; it stays server-side.
acceptance_criteria: Stats show aircraft count, positioned count, max observed range, and message rate if available; nothing rendered or served reveals the receiver position; the map default view is a fixed general region, not the antenna.
close_reason: Numbers-only session stats readout shipped (commit 0907293): server adsb.stats computes running max range from the private receiver position and message rate from a new ultrafeeder Metadata side-channel; wire envelope gains an allowlist-projected stats map (max-range-km + message-rate scalars only, no position/counts); client adsb.ui.stats renders/dashes them via a :stats/session sub. No receiver location anywhere in the browser. bb test (117 clj + 107 cljs) and bb lint clean.

title: Aircraft enrichment from a static aircraft database
description: Look up ICAO hex in a static database (tar1090-db style) to show type, registration, and operator in the detail panel and list. Decide where the DB lives (served by backend vs bundled) and treat it as data, not truth - enrichment must degrade to absent, never block the live path.
design:
ARCHITECTURE: Browser-side enrichment against static sharded JSON served by the backend as plain files (tar1090's own pattern). NO wire-format change — the SSE wire is untouched, so the live path never waits on a lookup and the concurrent wire.cljc edit never conflicts.

DB SOURCE: tar1090-db (github.com/wiedehopf/tar1090-db), data maintained by Mictronics (mictronics.de/aircraft-database). We fetch the single stable file aircraft.csv.gz from the csv branch: https://github.com/wiedehopf/tar1090-db/raw/refs/heads/csv/aircraft.csv.gz (~8MB gz). CSV is semicolon-delimited, no header, escapechar backslash, columns: icao;registration;typecode;flags;description;year;operator; (trailing empty). We use icao(0), registration(1), typecode(2), description(4), operator(6).

LICENSE: No LICENSE file in tar1090-db (all 404) nor a stated license on the Mictronics page (online search discontinued 'due to abuse'; data compiled from public registration sources). Treat as third-party data of UNSTATED license: DATA, not truth (validation-boundaries.md), fetched at build/dev time, NEVER committed (large + license-unclear), resources/public/db/ gitignored, everything degrades to absent when the dir is missing. Documented in README.

SHARD SCHEME (ours, not tar1090's native trie): deliberate deviation. tar1090's native db/ uses an ADAPTIVE split (blocklimit 24576) with a 'children' recursion + version drift (db vs db-2); reimplementing that lazy trie walk faithfully in CLJS is more code and more fragile than a fixed prefix we own. So a build-time babashka transform (bb db:fetch) reads aircraft.csv.gz and writes resources/public/db/<first-3-hex>.json, each a JSON object {icao -> {t,d,r,o}} (absent fields omitted). 3-char prefix keeps files small even in the dense US 'a' block. This is the bead's named fallback, adopted as primary and documented.

CLIENT (adsb.enrich): lazily fetches a hex's shard via js/fetch behind a redef-able seam (get-shard!), caches per-prefix in app-db :enrich/shards {prefix -> :loading|{icao->rec}|:absent}. :enrich/ensure event is idempotent (no-op if prefix known). Fetch failure OR malformed (non-map) JSON -> :absent, logged once. Sub :enrich/record [icao] is pure/side-effect-free (never fetches). Panel triggers ensure + shows Type/Registration/Operator rows (em-dash when unknown). Sidebar shows type code only from ALREADY-cached shards (cheap, no eager fetch). Non-6-hex / ~-prefixed icaos are never enriched. NEVER blocks or delays aircraft rendering — enrichment is orthogonal to the picture.
acceptance_criteria: A known hex shows type, registration, and operator in the panel and list; an unknown hex degrades to absent; the live path is unaffected when the DB is missing; the DB source and its license are documented.
close_reason: Browser-side enrichment against a static, backend-served sharded database (tar1090's own pattern); no SSE wire change, so the live path never waits and it degrades to absent when the DB is missing. adsb.enrich lazily fetches per-hex-prefix shards behind a redef-able seam, caches in app-db, and treats 404/network/malformed all as :absent (logged once); lookups are pure. bb db:fetch transforms tar1090-db's aircraft.csv.gz (Mictronics data, UNSTATED license — no LICENSE file, no stated terms) into resources/public/db/<abc>.json, gitignored and never committed. Panel shows Type/Registration/Operator rows (em-dash when unknown); sidebar shows the type code from already-cached shards only. Full bb test (117 clj + 119 cljs) + lint clean; db:fetch verified end-to-end (570,856 aircraft / 1,001 shards) and static serving + degradation proven. Commit 1a7ddb3.

Final act of the bead: docs/design-direction.md §11 reconciled with what actually shipped — item 5 no longer claims the map annotations are owed (adsb-bb1 delivered them), and the section now closes with 'nothing here is outstanding: the direction as written is the direction as built'. bb lint 0/0. No code touched in this step (doc only), so the suite was not re-run — the pass's own green run stands (144 clj/609 assertions, 166 cljs/859).

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: Overseer finding from the dgb.10 pick (2026-07-12): Annotation crowned for legibility over Marginalia and looks over Drafting Room — BUT at the smallest sizes Drafting Room still reads slightly easier. Diagnosis: B's smallest labels are caps-tracked upright humanist sans (open apertures, no italic); Annotation's smallest voice is italic mono, the first thing to strain when small. Implication for the visual pass: where the smallest Annotation labels strain, prefer the plotter's PRINTED voice (caps-tracked bold upright mono — the treatment its block headings already use) over the scribbled italic. Auteur has offered to stage this as an A/B in #/preview before dgb.5 commits to it.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: Caption voice landed while you were between commits (Overseer pick, bead adsb-fon): small labels now print in Space Grotesk 500 caps-tracked 0.12em at --t-2 via ONE grouped rule near the end of app.css's chrome section (.adsb-count-unit/.adsb-stats-label/.adsb-fact-label/.adsb-stack-shelf-label — their own blocks keep only colour/geometry; the italic declarations were removed). §5 has a new caption-voice clause; the preview's Captions rack defaults to printed-grotesk. Grotesk stays caps-labels-only by law — never titles, body, or data. Font: fonts/space-grotesk-500.woff2 (OFL), @font-face in app.css.

title: Decide the design direction
description: archetypes/AUTEUR.md previously referenced /docs/design-direction.md and /docs/design-questionnaire.md — both carried over from the old project and NEITHER EXISTS. Rather than invent the Overseer's taste, AUTEUR now says plainly that no direction is settled and instructs him to propose rather than assume. When the Overseer settles on one, write docs/design-direction.md and restore the reference in AUTEUR.
acceptance_criteria: docs/design-direction.md exists and records the Overseer's decision; the reference in archetypes/AUTEUR.md is restored; adsb-dgb.5 (visual design pass) is unblocked.
notes:
Overseer answered the questionnaire 2026-07-12. VERDICT: base = THE SECTIONAL, with three amendments and two commissioned inventions. Answers: 1b 2c 3c 4c 5(a,b,c,d) 6a+shadow 7a 8=none-of-the-above 9c 10a 11a 12c 13c 14=DEFERRED 15c.

AMENDMENTS TO SECTIONAL AS DRAWN:
(1) Must survive the dark - Q2c follows the system theme, so the artifact needs a genuine NIGHT EDITION, not a filter over the warm-paper day one. Biggest risk in the direction; prototype first.
(2) It breathes - Q10a/Q11a overturn Sectional's 'chrome holds perfectly still': glows pulse, panels ease, always-on fading trails for EVERY aircraft. Nocturne's pulse inside Sectional's body.
(3) Quiet surface, deep axis - Q7a kills Sectional's always-on annotation data blocks (glyphs only, callsign on hover). The sidebar information moves to the Stack.

TWO INVENTIONS NO CANDIDATE HAD (both load-bearing, both need a prototype not a mockup):
- CAST SHADOW (Q6): keep the continuous gradient for precision, but every aircraft throws a shadow onto the chart with offset+blur scaling with altitude. Height becomes physical; vertical rate rides the same channel (closing shadow = descending). Rejected: contour halo rings (noisy at density), leader-line length (fights Q7a).
- THE STACK (Q8): NO SIDEBAR. A live altitude ruler down one edge; every aircraft is a tick at its true flight level, drifting as it climbs. It is the altitude legend, the altitude scale, and the aircraft list in one object - a profile view of the sky beside the plan view. Hover lights the plane, click selects. On phone it lies down as a horizontal strip (Q9c: neither stage degrades). Rejected: ATC flight-progress strip rack (may return as the SELECTION detail surface), chart-marginalia panel.

SCHEDULED, NOT DECIDED: Q14 typography/scales/palette - Overseer wants a bake-off against the real layout, not a decision in the abstract.

NEXT: write docs/design-direction.md from this, restore the reference in archetypes/AUTEUR.md.
close_reason: Direction settled and committed (292f84c). docs/design-direction.md records the verdict rendered from the answered questionnaire (a7dc144): THE SECTIONAL, DAY & NIGHT — Sectional base per Q1b/Q3c/Q4c/Q12c/Q15c, amended per the Overseer: (1) two printed editions following the system theme, night edition prototyped FIRST as the top risk; (2) Nocturne's motion inside Sectional's body — glides, always-on trails, easing chrome, soft pulses, but the emergency treatment never flashes; (3) quiet surface — glyphs only, callsign on hover, NO sidebar. Two commissioned inventions specced and gated behind prototype beads: the cast shadow (altitude as shadow offset/softness) and the Stack (live altitude ruler = list + legend + scale, vertical on desktop, horizontal on phone). Typography deferred to a bake-off bead against the real layout. Full day+night palettes with hex, basemap = recolored OpenFreeMap Liberty variants (provider from adsb-kh4.5), aircraft styling mapped onto src/cljs/adsb/map/style.cljs constants, emergency grave-but-unmissable (red pen circle, MAYDAY stamp, NOTAM strip, edge arrow, no camera hijack), receiver never shown, phone/desktop equal. archetypes/AUTEUR.md reference restored; overturned across-the-room glanceability bullet removed (Q15c). Ordered execution brief for adsb-dgb.5 in §11 — visual pass unblocked.

title: Schema vocabulary for airspeed/heading and GNSS altitude
description: adsb.ingest.mode-s decodes TC19 subtype 3/4 airspeed (IAS/TAS) and magnetic heading in full (airborne-velocity, :velocity/* keys) but the delta cannot carry them: adsb.schema has no :aircraft/airspeed or heading fields, and mapping heading onto :aircraft/track-deg would be a lie. Similarly TC20-22 GNSS height (HAE) currently lands in :aircraft/altitude-ft because the schema speaks one altitude vocabulary, where dump1090 keeps alt_baro/alt_geom distinct. When the schema grows this vocabulary, extend velocity-delta and altitude-ft in adsb.ingest.mode-s (the decode is already done and tested) and wire.cljc/UI as needed.
